Lore (Battle Royale)

Contents

  • 1 Glossary
  • 2 Major Storyline Characters
  • 3 Major Storyline Factions
  • 4 Storyline
  • 5 References
  • 6 See Also

Overview

This article is about the lore of Battle Royale. For the lore of Save the World, see Lore (Save the World). For the timeline of changes and events in the Fortnite storyline, see Timeline.
This page may contain speculation and/or fan interpretations.
Due to the lack of official information, some info on this page may therefore be partially incorrect.

Fortnite: Battle Royale has Lore (storyline/narrative/stories) which has evolved throughout the Seasons and is present in map changes, Quests, live events, cinematic experiences, comics and more.

Glossary

Glossary[]

Glossary
Image Term Description
The Nothing An Omniversal dark force that has existed since before the Zero Point and eeking to destroy The Omniverse.
The Zero Point The source of all Realities in The Omniverse. It was broken and is now scattered into several Shards after The Big Bang.
Reality Zero The Reality of Fortnite: Battle Royale before Chapter 5 which contained the Zero Point.
The Loop The 22-minute matches of Battle Royale, in which Loopers lose their memory, their ability to speak and are forced to eliminate each other.
Live Events Gameplay and cinematic experiences that usually happen at the end of a Season to conclude its story and introduce the next Season.
Rifts Rifts are portals allowing space-time travel. There are two types of Rifts: Pure Portals and Sideways Rifts. Pure Portals are energy portals originating from the Zero Point and are therefore pre-attuned to it and the Island, Agent Jones used this way to travel into different realities. Sideways Rifts are space-time anomalies independent of the Zero Point that connect two or more realities, points in space or time periods. The Seven, Fox Clan and Kado Thorne use them to travel through realities, space and time.
Cubes Dark and corruptive cubes belonging to The Last Reality.
Chrome A living, liquid metal mastered by The Herald with ties to The Last Reality and The Nothing. Chrome consumes and destroys.
Snapshots Duplicates of Loopers who escape The Loop.
Timeline A timeline, or alternate timeline, is a world, a distinct reality within the Omniverse, that branches off from another reality at a specific moment. This split can occur naturally or as the result of time-travel interference, creating a new reality that exists in parallel with the original. The reality depicted in Chapter 4: Season OG is an alternate timeline of Reality Zero. Although it is presented as a version of the past, the events within it did not rewrite Reality Zero's history; instead, they unfolded simultaneously, existing alongside Reality Zero as a parallel reality.
Pandora's Box The catalyst of all evil in Chapter 5 coming from The Pantheon's home reality.
Major Storyline Characters

Major Storyline Characters[]

List of Major Storyline Characters
Image Faction Description
The Looper Character that players embody and main protagonist of Fortnite. They are a very powerful fighter who saved the Island countless times and even led the fight against The Pantheon. After The Underground was disbanded, they followed Hope and Jones to Reality: 783 in order to help them.
Geno Fortnite's overarching antagonist until Chapter 3. The founder and leader of the Imagined Order with a vision to bring Order to Everything, perfect every Reality in the Omniverse and create the ideal civilization. He subjugated The Zero Point for thousands of years to trap people in The Loop for research.
Doctor Slone A former member of the Inner Circle of the Imagined Order and leader of the Island Management Team. She was a major antagonist, before becoming a protagonist.
John Jones Former member of the Imagined Order's Island Management Team who betrayed them for the Seven. After the war against Doom he left Helios alongside Hope to go in Reality: 783 and search for an artifact similar to Pandora's Box. Although it wasn't originally planned, he teams up again with The Looper.
Peely Humanoid banana, member of the Resistance and The Underground. He now goes by the identity of Peelverine.

Agent Jones' best friend.

The Foundation The leader of The Seven, who saved the island multiple times, such as in the Zero Crisis Finale. He went on to defeat The Imagined Order by leading The Resistance.
The Paradigm One of The Seven. She is The Seven's pilot who piloted the Mecha in Chapter 1. She was deemed a traitor by the rest of The Seven because she helped the Imagined Order build the Mecha.
The Imagined One of The Seven. Geno's genetically modified daughter.
The Order One of The Seven. Geno's second genetically modified daughter.
The Origin One of The Seven. He was previously King of the Cubes, but escaped and was imprisoned by the IO. The Seven reached out a hand and he joined them as their field general and tactician.
The Scientist One of The Seven, their handsome engineer.

He is responsible for The End (Chapter 1) and was one the protagonists of great events in Chapter 3, until being chromed and existentially resculpted by The Nothing.

The Visitor One of The Seven. He is their navigational expert who blasted off Fortnite's lore.
The Visitor One of an alternate iteration of The Seven, from a distant reality. He assisted Reality Zero's Seven in their mission against the Ice King and the Dark Voyager. Following the creation of the Shattered Coast, his alliances are questioned, and his true identity is not what it seems.
AMIE The Scientist's enigmatic AI who managed to survive even after Fracture. After those events, after receiving a signal from The Scientist, she went to look for him to save him from a dark fate.
The Cube Queen Ruler of The Last Reality.
The Herald The Herald of The Nothing and the master of Chrome.
Midas Midas is an international crime boss who seeks conquest through espionage. He is the founder and leader of Midas' crew.
Mecha Was built by the Imagined Order, Singularity and The Paradigm in Season 9 to defeat The Devourer. It was repaired in Chapter 3: Season 2 to destroy The Collider.
Kado Thorne Kado Thorne is an ancient vampire of The Eclipse and the leader of the Eclipse faction. He is a space time traveler, a thief and a wealthy collector. He travels through space and time, stealing items from different realities and from time periods and leaving behind a calling card and a portrait of the Eclipse.
The Oracle A former mortal and seer connected to The Pantheon, reborn as an oracle by Apollo. She previously teamed up with Hope, Jones, The Looper and The Underground to defeat Doom
Hope One of the main protagonists after Chapter 5, former leader of The Underground, Valeria's sister and protagonist of a The Pantheon prophecy, as well as Jones' partner and Pandora's Chosen. She left Helios alongside Jones to go in Reality: 783 and search for an artifact similar to Pandora's Box. Although it wasn't originally planned, she teams up again with The Looper, she then leaves after defeating the Dark Presence to the Golden Coast
Megalo Don Ruler of the Nitro Wasteland, who seeks to fuel destruction. Former leader of the Wasteland Warriors and master of the mighty Leviathan convoy. Former ally of the mysterious Wanderer (Doctor Doom), who once aided him in mining Nitro from Pandora’s Box. He was “got rid of” by Doom, now presumed deceased.
Doctor Victor Von Doom Ruler of the Kingdom of Doom, who desires a world of perfect order. Former ally of the Imagined Order, and later of Megalo Don under the name of "The Wanderer". Once his true identity is revealed and the powers of Pandora's Box are absorbed, he is stronger than ever, killing various characters, including Magneto.
Iron Man One of Marvel's heroes who helped us defeat Galactus in Chapter 2: Season 4 and later the Imagined Order in Collision. He later returned in Chapter 5: Season 4 to defeat Doom alongside The Underground.
The Night Rose One of the residents of Oninoshima in Reality: 783. The Night Rose is a demon who was once bound by The Puppeteer against her will. Throughout the study of the spirit realm and quest to retrieve Jade from the realm she is freed, later joining Jones and Hope with her set on revenge. She continues as their ally later becoming a teacher at Supernova Academy.
Kendo One of the residents of Oninoshima in Reality: 783. He is a samurai who teamed up and formed a close bond with Hope in the goal of protecting his home. He is the younger brother of Daigo, older brother of Jade, and a descendant of the family that caused the Shattering War.
Jade One of the residents of Oninoshima in Reality: 783. She is a guardian of the Sprites and all the magic in her home and a main target of the Demon Warriors. She is the younger sister of both Kendo and Daigo, and a descendant of the family that caused the Shattering War.
Daigo One of the residents of Oninoshima in Reality: 783. Formerly turned into a corrupted commander of the Demon Warriors, he has been returned to normal and now defends his home alongside his family. He is the older brother of both Kendo and Jade, and a descendant of the family that caused the Shattering War.
Dark Voyager One of the members of The Army of the Last Reality, serving as an explorer. He arrived on Reality: 266 and mysteriously shot down the Battle Bus with his personal spaceship. Jones suggested he is a herald of something else.
Major Storyline Factions

Major Storyline Factions[]

Main Article: Factions
List of Major Storyline Factions
Image Faction Description
Imagined Order Fortnite's main antagonists (alongside The Last Reality) until Chapter 3: Season 2. They're an ancient organization founded by Geno who have operated in The Bridge for thousands of years, trapping people in The Loop for research as part of their grand plan to bring Order to Everything... However, their schemes to intertwine worlds have disrupted the natural course of development for many Realities.
Midas' Crew A major organization in Fortnite, they serve as both antagonists or protagonists or both at the same time depending on the situation. Midas' Crew is a criminal organization mainly composed of spies. Its main purpose is to serve the various schemes of Midas, notably in his goal of conquest through intrigue and espionage. However, they also sell their services to various clients such as Spillexx, the Imagined Order or Snoop's Enterprise. The organization is separated into three distinct factions: GHOST, SHADOW and neutral, with The Outlaws temporarily being under Midas' command as well.
The Seven Fortnite's main protagonists until Chapter 4. A group of seven with a plan to destroy the IO and The Loop and free The Zero Point.
The Resistance The Loopers' resistance against the Imagined Order in Chapter 3: Season 2, led by The Seven.
The Last Reality Fortnite's main antagonists - an evil army that destroys Realities, leaving Nothing to remain. Their army is comprised of aliens, Cubes, Chrome and more.
Demon Hunters/The Supernova Academy Fortnite Chapter 6's main protagonists, created to quell the Demonic threat and secure the Zero Shard alongside the Spirit Siblings. Following the Death Star Sabotage, the team increased in number by officially founding the Supernova Academy, meant to train young superheroes. Later, they joined force with the O.X.R., sharing their assets to fight against the Demon Warriors.
O.X.R. Fortnite Chapter 6 Season 4's main protagonists, it's an old military organization founded generations ago to fight and annihilate The Swarm, a particular insectoid species of demons part of the Spirit Realm's dark forces. They joined force with The Supernova Academy against The Swarm and the Dark Presence.
Demon Warriors Fortnite's Chapter 6's main antagonists - an evil army of countless demons from the Spirit Realm under the orders of the Dark Presence. Although their master's motives are little known, they are a very dangerous threat to realities, similarly to The Last Reality.
Storyline

Storyline[]

Main Article: Timeline
  • Prologue
  • Reality Zero Saga
  • Big Bang Saga
  • Dark Voyager Saga
  • Side Stories

The Big Bang[]

In the beginning, there was Nothing. Then, in a burst of pure creation, The Zero Point sprang into being. Countless realities originated from The Zero Point, each its own universe, with its own multiverse - and as a whole, an Omniverse. In real life, this event is known as the 'Big Bang'. The location of The Zero Point is Reality Zero, the origin reality containing The Zero Point.

The IO Rises[]

Over 1000 years ago, Geno discovered The Zero Point. He founded the Imagined Order to control and enslave it underneath an artificial Island. With its power, they travelled to different Realities, hiring more scientists. Scientists who joined the Island Management Team would be granted immortality, and their home worlds would be protected by the IO. Agent Jones was the first to jump into The Zero Point to see what happened, creating his first Snapshot, Jonesy The First, on The Island. The IO used The Zero Point to travel to different Realities in their plan to perfect them and create the ideal civilization, trapping people in The Island's Loop for research, disastrously affecting the development of worlds.

The Loop[]

They utilize The Loop, a 22 minute time loop that is used to trap anyone who arrives on the Island, erasing their memory and rendering them unable to speak. The Loop uses The Storm to push combatants together over the 22 minute period, eventually closing all the way in and eliminating anyone trapped in it. If someone is to be the last standing in the loop, at the exact closing point of the storm, they will escape the loop, and wake up in the same spot they left, with all of the Loop's effects gone, yet still trapped on the Island. Eventually, The Imagined Order began experimenting with The Loop, kidnapping people from different realities and trapping them on the island, where they fought again and again in a Battle Royale. The IO also creates Bunker Hatches to The Bridge. Geno sends his daughters into the Loop, confident that they were so perfect, they would be the first to escape the loop. When his daughters finally manage to escape the loop, Geno had already replaced them with stronger Snapshots of themselves.

The Ice Moon[]

On the Ice Moon, The Ice Kingdom, led by The Ice King, travelled across realities with allies to collect powerful technology, artifacts, and beings too dangerous to exist. When The Ice King's staunchest comrade argued to use their collected items to alter realities to their liking, The Ice King froze him and all his knights in his fortress. The Ice King brought The Ice Moon to Reality Zero before freezing himself to sleep forever, leaving The Ice Moon to orbit Reality Zero.

The Cube King's End[]

The Cube King left the Last Reality but was captured by the Imagined Order. They abuse him for knowledge about The Last Reality. The Cube Queen becomes the ruler of The Last Reality.

The Seven's Formation[]

A group of seven members form together with the aim of destroying the IO, and liberating The Zero Point. The Foundation is the leader of the group. The Cube King joins as The Origin and Geno's daughters join as The Imagined and The Order. The other members join, better known as The Visitor, The Scientist, and The Paradigm. The Seven fear Geno, as he and the Imagined Order, with the control of The Zero Point, can cause potential catastrophic damage to The Omniverse as a whole. The Seven develop sealed environment suits as a way to enter and leave The Loop anytime they want without being affected by the Loop's effects. The Paradigm left the Seven and joined the IO temporarily.

The First Shadows[]

Midas set his sights on building an empire of espionage, intrigue, and conquest. He founded SHADOW.

The Shattering War[]

In Reality: 783 on Oninoshima Island, the River Guard and the Fox Clan were etablished. The latter were in possession of powerful magic masks allowing them to travel realities. However, the mask maker the Fox Clan, becoming too ambitious, created the Masks of Power, powerful and magical Oni Masks that threatened the whole Island. From this, the first demons appeared and a war broke out between the Island and the demonic forces, the event known as the Shattering War, resulting in the the destruction of most of the masks, the sealing of demons in the Spirit Realm and the banishment of magic on the Island. The Fox Clan were blamed for the entire incident, and they and the River Guard became sworn enemies.

  • Chapter 1
  • Chapter 2
  • Chapter 3
  • Chapter 4

Pre-Season

Pre-Season[]

The Looper[]

The main character that the player controls, only dubbed "The Looper" by The Foundation, gets trapped in the Loop and joins the battle royale against other combatants.

Season 1

Season 1[]

The first steps in The Loop shape its fighters, and as the 22 minutes repeat endlessly, Renegade Raider is among those who win their freedom by being the last one standing at the center of The Storm.

Season 2

Season 2[]

Amidst the combat, new knights claim lands of the Island: Black Knight as the odious scourge of Wailing Woods, Red Knight as the red menace of Anarchy Acres, Blue Squire as the bold warrior of Moisty Mire and Royale Knight as the dauntless champion of Tomato Town.

Season 3

Season 3[]

A comet appears in the sky above the island. It gradually gets closer over time. During the final weeks of the season, Small meteorites begin to land on the island as a precursor to the large meteor. Players theorized that The Comet would destroy Tilted Towers, as the inhabitants of the island who had previously escaped The Loop began observing the mysterious object with telescopes dotted around the island.

Season 4

Season 4[]

New Characters: The Visitor, Omega, Carbide

The Impact[]

The Comet finally slams into the island, crashing into the factories near Salty Springs, destroying Dusty Depot almost completely and creating a gigantic crater in the ground, scattered with hop rocks. Other, smaller comet fragments land around the island, damaging Tilted Towers, The Prison, and Risky Reels. At the center of the main crater, now called Dusty Divot, The Government sets up a research station to monitor a mysterious rock at the center of the crater. The mysterious organization takes over the island, and begins to patch up the smaller craters around the island and rebuild.

Lights, Camera, Action[]

A film production is started about a supervillain; Omega escaping from a prison that was hit by a meteor, forming a robot army of Chromiums and Diecasts, and destroying the heroes using the rocket. Carbide and his team of superheroes fight against Omega in the final showdown of the film.

The Visitor[]

The Visitor, a member of The Seven, dedicated to wiping out the IO, is revealed to be in stasis in a pod within the Dusty Divot rock. Eventually, he awakens and breaks out, trashing the facility and making his way to Villain Lair, located in the mountains near Snobby Shores as a prop for the upcoming movie being filmed, which actually contained a fully-functioning rocket.


The Blast Off[]

“
Zero Point Oscillator on, Setting coordinates. Engaging quantum field accelerator..
”
— The Visitor

While the movie was being filmed, the Government collects and moves all of the Hop Rocks to the Villain Lair from each of the craters in a convoy of trucks. The Visitor takes over the base, and modifies the Rocket to be able to traverse realities using the hop rocks. The Visitor launches the rocket, opening temporary rifts to teleport around the island. Eventually, the rocket boosts into the sky, creating a gigantic Sideways Rift leading to an unknown destination. This event destabilized Reality Zero, and left it vulnerable to future invaders.

Situation Worsens[]

As Reality Zero continues to destabilize, rifts around the island begin opening to other Realities, bringing and taking props or even people, to and from Reality Zero. The Sideways Rift grows larger, as it brings and takes more items from Realities. Several Supply Llamas are rifted to various places on Earth, including Paris, Cologne, Cannes, Warsaw, and London. The Durrr Burger Mascot was rifted into Antelope Valley, California, and was the start of the Season 5 ARG. Several objects were brought to the island instead, including an ancient wagon, dinosaur bones, and... a ship anchor?

Season 5

Season 5[]

New Characters: Drift, Brite Bomber, Cuddle Team Leader, Rex, Enforcers, Kevin the Cube

Worlds Collide[]

The Sideways Rift pools massive amounts of matter to Reality Zero from other realities, bringing in Paradise Palms, The Desert, Lazy Links, and Viking Village to the island, but destroying Moisty Mires and Anarchy Acres in the process. Smaller rifts are clustered around the changed areas, as space-time in those areas are recovering from the shifts. Drift is rifted to Reality Zero after painting graffiti on the Durr Burger, and is caught in the transition when the burger is sent back to the island.

Road Trip[]

Drift and his new friends Cuddle Team Leader, Brite Bomber and Rex travel around the Island, meeting more people participating in activities. They visit the The Burger, then Lazy Links, then the Race Track, then the Dinosaur statues, then finally Risky Reels.


Fox Clan's Target[]

During the road trip, Drift gets a mask from a stranger, making him a member of the Fox Clan, but also a target for a mysterious group of people. Drift progresses throughout his stages as the weeks go on.

Balance and Order[]

As Reality Zero starts to heal, and the Sideways Rift starts to close, The Imagined Order send The Enforcers to the island, who aim to restore balance and order. They arrive through rifts and return objects that previously vanished through interdimensional travel back to the island.


The Cube Emerges[]

As the Sideways Rift continued to rapidly close up, it suddenly turned purple. Within the next few days, purple storm lightning bolts began shooting out of the crack, all targeting a hill near Paradise Palms, containing a large stone rock surrounded by a ring of cacti. Each lightning strike picked off one cacti one-by-one, until a larger lightning bolt destroyed the large rock. The Crack Closure event occurred on 24th August 2018, with a gigantic storm lightning bolt striking out of the rift, lasting for around 10 seconds. When it was over, the Crack in the Sky closed. then The Cube was formed. Though it first appeared to be a stationary object, it began to move at a rate of one revolution every 1 hour and 43 minutes.

During its journey around Athena, The Cube printed seven runes with anti-gravity domes surrounding.

After finishing this process, The Cube destroyed one of the buildings in Tilted Towers and then proceeded to the edge of Loot Lake on September 19, 2018. The Cube appeared to dissolve, turning Loot Lake in a purple-pink gelatinous state with properties akin to The Cube itself, such as becoming bouncy.


Season 6

Season 6[]

New Characters: Calamity, Deadfire, A.I.M.

Darkness Rises[]

With the beginning of Season 6, the Cube—now fully energized—rose from the waters of Loot Lake, lifting the cabin and the island at its center with it to form the Floating Island. This ascent created a massive hole in the lake, exposing the bridge leading to The Zero Point, which appeared as a vortex at its center.

Just two days after the island began to rise, the Cube started moving the Floating Island to each of the runes it had imprinted during Season 5. After absorbing energy from all the runes, the Cube and the island descended back to Loot Lake. Kevin then began to power The Storm itself by channeling energy into the sky, forming a portal-like phenomenon that continued to expand until it enveloped the entire island.[1] On October 24, 2018—the first day of Fortnitemares 2018—the Cube released a massive burst of energy, causing the Floating Island to explode into multiple fragments. In the aftermath, the Storm began forming Cube-like monoliths across the island and spawning Cube Monsters.

The In-Between[]

However, the Loopers led by Calamity repeatedly and relentlessly attacked the Cube, causing it to begin cracking. After sustaining heavy damage, the Cube started leaking its dark nectar into the Vortex, drip by drip, until the final drop fell. On November 4, 2018, the Cube began spinning rapidly before erupting in a blinding white flash, splitting itself apart. Following the flash, the Loopers were transported to The In-Between. Shortly afterward, a rift formed and manifested as a butterfly, which approached The Looper and landed gently on their finger. The butterfly then opened a Rift, returning the Loopers to Athena and restoring Leaky Lake.

A Mysterious Tracker[]

A.I.M., sent by Sgt. Winter, lands on Flush Factory, plants a flag, and observes the loopers in preparation for an invasion. An Iceberg, a fragment of the The Ice Moon rapidly approaches the Island, with Expedition and The Ice King on it.


Season 7

Season 7[]

New Characters: The Ice King, The Prisoner, Sgt. Winter, Lynx

Within the Snow[]

Sgt. Winter and his army, with the Iceberg which broke off The Ice Moon, arrive onto Athena, with Frosty Flights as their headquarters. They bring X-4 Stormwings and set up more bases around Athena. A giant iceberg; Polar Peak appears with a castle on the top, holding The Ice King dormant inside, frozen inside ice.

Weather Warning[]

The ice begins to melt due to winter coming to an end, and it wakes up The Ice King, but also reveals a mysterious figure trapped inside ice in the castle. Ice King acquires a shard of a Cube, and uses it to cover the entire Island with snow. The snowstorm also caused the invasion of Ice Monsters, due to the combination of the cube's and the Ice King's powers.

The Prisoner[]

The snow slowly melts again, releasing The Prisoner who was trapped in the castle. He begins breaking free from his chains and regaining his true form, with his final step being a ritual at Wailing Woods. This would also be the beginning of the Earthquakes, as foreshadowing for the Volcano. The earthquakes created fissures in the ground, one of them destroying News HQ in Tilted Towers.

Season 8

Season 8[]

New Characters: Hybrids, Blackheart, Master Key

The King of Fire[]

The Prisoner creates a Volcano inside a jungle, and hatches the eggs he stole from Polar Peak that eventually grow up to be Hybrids. They go after Master Key for unknown reasons. The group of Peelys would then hold a Ripening Ritual.

"They" Return[]

The Government set up excavation sites around the island searching for The Vault, being unsuccessful in their first few excavations. A mysterious metal plate is discovered near Loot Lake. All the excavators move there, setting up a considerably larger site which eventually reveals the Vault in the middle.

The Unvaulting[]

Five Runes appear around the map, with each needing a specific task for the Loopers to do in order for them to move. After all of the runes were placed, The Vault's hatch opened, allowing Loopers to go inside. Inside the Vault, numerous Vaulted Items were stored inside crystal pillars. A desk with Singularity's helmet was also inside the Vault, along with a radio and a few books. The Loopers start to crack the items' cases with their Harvesting Tools, with the Drum Gun's case breaking first. It was then released from the Vault, along with all the Loopers inside.

While The Unvaulting was happening, The Prisoner erupts the Volcano, sending balls of magma to Retail Row and Tilted Towers which completely destroys them. Polar Peak was also shot, creating a huge crack in the side of the iceberg. Afterwards, The Ice King would freeze The Prisoner underwater for all time before eventually returning to The Ice Moon.

Season 9

Season 9[]

New Characters: Singularity, Bunker Jonesy, The Devourer, Mecha Team Leader, The Paradigm

Bunker Days[]

During the Eruption, Jonesy and Peely escape into a Bunker in Tilted Towers, enjoying the various activities inside. Once they went to go eat, it is revealed they have no food. Jonesy turns Peely into a Smoothie, and drank his juice to survive. After an unknown amount of time, Sentinel opens up the Bunker, and welcomes Jonesy and Peely into Neo Tilted.

Welcome The Future[]

After Tilted Towers and Retail Row were destroyed, They are rebuilt as Neo Tilted and Mega Mall. The Vault has been overgrown, and turned into an energy source for Neo Tilted using The Zero Point, with Loot Lake becoming a Government base. The now-dormant Volcano has turned into Pressure Plant.

The Monster[]

Inside the hole in Polar Peak, the eye of The Devourer, which later breaks out and starts destroying nearby landscape and locations, and escaping to the ocean with Polar Peak on it's back. On the other side, at Pressure Plant, a giant elevator is built, and parts of a giant mech are put together piece by piece, with it eventually being finished and colored, becoming the Mecha Team Leader. Singularity helps build the mech while The Paradigm is allowed to pilot it as a chance to battle The Devourer in case it poses a threat to The Zero Point.

The Final Showdown[]

The Devourer rises from the ocean, destroying buildings with it's laser beam. It heads towards Loot Lake as The Mecha Team Leader activates, flying towards The Devourer. After a fight, The Devourer rips off Mecha's arm, tossing it into the ocean. It heads to The Vault to reach The Zero Point, but before it could, Mecha blows The Devourer away. It then took The Zero Point out of The Vault, cracking it to get power to use against The Devourer. It then pulls out the Statue of Singularity from Neo Tilted, which turns out to be a giant sword. The Devourer dashed towards Mecha as Mecha lunges, stabbing the sword into The Devourer's head, killing it. Mecha celebrated with a Floss, before flying off to The Ice Moon. The Zero Point was left over The Vault, destabilizing more every day.

Season X

Season X[]

New Characters: The Scientist

“
What the Seven of us are attempting is very risky. I suspect They are not the only ones watching. But it must be done or we lose the Bridge forever.
”
— The Scientist

Out of Time[]

After The Zero Point became unstable, it released a burst of energy along with a Reality Wave, converting Dusty Divot into Dusty Depot. The explosion is frozen by the arrival of The Scientist's meteor just above Dusty Depot. At the start of the season his pod was already open, meaning he was already on the Island.

The Scientist[]

The Scientist takes over the grey warehouse in Dusty Depot, and turns it into his workspace. He starts building Rift Beacons, used to create a Sideways Rift over a Location to replace it with it's version from an alternate universe.

Behind the scenes, the IO manipulate The Seven into stabilizing The Zero Point, so they don't lose The Bridge and the control they have over it. Despite being enemies, The Scientist still helps the IO, but unknown if willingly. Neither the Seven nor the IO wanted to lose the bridge, but IO left the Seven to do all the work.

Rift Zones[]

The Scientist's Rift Beacons create Rift Zones, which swaps locations with alternate reality counterparts, each with it's unique rules inside. The Scientist first turns Neo Tilted into Tilted Town, Mega Mall back into Retail Row, Paradise Palms into Moisty Palms, Frozen Greasy Grove back into Greasy Grove, and the former location of the destroyed Starry Suburbs into Starry Suburbs. He accidentally destroys the Sports Center, and creates a crater with glitching objects. He also brings back the Floating Island, with more additions like the Motel sign and the The Burger.

The End
[]

“
Activate the beacon at precisely the moment this timer reaches nothing. The Zero Point must be contained once more. If They are correct, it will be the end.
”
— The Scientist

The Scientist builds a Rocket at Dusty Depot, and launches it, with 6 more Rockets originating from the Rift Zones. All of them create a Rift in front of the frozen Meteor, which pushes it into the rift. A rift appears over Loot Lake which a Rocket emerges from, with the Meteor following behind it. All the Loopers are launched into the sky, after the initial rocket hits The Zero Point's bubble. The Meteor then hits the bubble, throwing players further away. It struggles to break through, eventually crashing into The Zero Point, creating the Black Hole, which destroys the island and sucks all of its matter into it.

Chapter 2: Season 1

Chapter 2: Season 1[]

[Redacted: D4][]

“
The Containment Field has stabilized; The Bridge is intact. The seven succeeded. Our manipulations averted disaster. The zero point is ours, and the plan can continue.
”
— Loading Messages

The Black Hole explodes into The Zero Point once again and releases Apollo, a brand new island created from the matter of the old island within the black hole. The Imagined Order sends in the E.G.O. alias the [Redacted: D4] team to scout the area after they arrive at Apollo through the Crash Site. They travel the whole Island, with IO expecting "similar embedded agents" as a threat against them.

A New World[]

After the Blackout, Athena reorganized into Apollo and Artemis, with Artemis being on the other side of Apollo. Apollo includes locations from Athena, such as Retail Row and Salty Springs. The Bridge is still intact, and the Seven take control of Artemis leaving the IO alone on Apollo. The reforming of the Island also attracts the attention of Galactus in Reality: 616, who travel a rift to arrive in Reality Zero at a later point in time.

E.G.O. Vs. A.L.T.E.R.[]

Unbeknownst to GHOST at the time, SHADOW had already begun engaging in various operations on the island. A.L.T.E.R. team members present on Apollo before E.G.O.'s interference are involved in the trade and marketing of weapons at Dirty Docks[2], and have established links with the companies Slurp Co.[3] and Kevolution Energy.[4]

Chaos Agent watches and masterminds everything from his secret lair on the goings-on between A.L.T.E.R./SHADOW and E.G.O./GHOST. He is interested in the REDACTED Bunker[5]. He also experiments with Sludges, and builds an army.

For Unspecified reason, A.L.T.E.R. go on a hunt for Rippley in an operation led by Toxin and an army of Sludges. Cameo saves Rippley in Frenzy Farm. In the meantime the rest of the [Redacted: D4] team locate Scratch and 8-Ball go after him. Toxin and Sludges follow E.G.O to the dam near Hydro 16, but they escape using a Motorboat. After the incident, Midas selects Eye Land as the new grounds for the main base of GHOST, likely aware of the Zero Point stationed underneath it. Simultaneously, construction on five SHADOW Safe Houses around the island begins.

Chapter 2: Season 2

Chapter 2: Season 2[]

Masters of Espionage[]

Midas founded The Agency, his employment organization for spies from both the factions of GHOST and SHADOW. The organization has its headquarters of the same name built on Eye Land. Conflicts between SHADOW and GHOST escalate, and grow into an all-out Spy War. Several spy bases were in operation across Apollo at this time: The Agency, The Grotto, The Shark, The Rig, and The Yacht. Besides the safehouses, however, there were no major compounds with public allegiance to SHADOW.

Spy War[]

With The Agency now built, and tension arisen between the two, the war between GHOST and SHADOW begins in full. While Meowscles controlled The Yacht for GHOST, there were three members of Midas' crew who had not yet chosen their allegiance and remained neutral at the beginning: Brutus, TNTina and Skye. However, over time, the balance of power between GHOST and SHADOW changed enormously:

  • Brutus was the first operative to choose SHADOW, and it became their The Grotto became the faction's Headquarters.
  • TNTina also joined SHADOW and was primarily responsible for the ownership and destruction of The Rig, spilling oil into the ocean.
  • Unexpectedly, Meowscles decided to betray GHOST for SHADOW, causing GHOST to send in Contract Giller and Deadpool to reclaim The Yacht for them. Meowscles, now a SHADOW operative, was then sent to the Box Factory.
    • Deadpool was granted authority for GHOST over The Yacht in Meowscles' absence.
  • Skye was the last member of Midas' main crew to choose a side, ultimately deciding to stick with GHOST. The Shark was eventually transformed into a prison HQ for GHOST.

Storm The Agency[]

Close to the end of the season, Renegade Shadow disguises as a GHOST spy and attempts to raid The Agency by eliminating GHOST Henchmen and taking over The Agency for SHADOW. His attempt was unsuccessful.

The Device[]

“
Wait, wait, wait. hold on... "It's not just a storm?" What does that mean?
Must be connected to the loop, but there's no way we could have predicted that it would react This way.
”
— Agent Jones

Secretly during the war, Midas gradually worked on a Doomsday Device with his daughter Jules made to break free from The Storm. This Device was located in his room, directly underneath The Agency, and possibly right above The Zero Point.

After The Device is activated, it pushes back The Storm, in an attempt to destroy The Loop. The Looper gets sent to The Bridge several times due to the use of the Device, although they are quickly sent back after each time. The Storm fights back against Midas' device, eventually being completely pushed back. The Looper are sent to the Bridge once again, where the panicking agents of the Imagined Order can be heard, before being sent back. Upon returning, the Storm has created a massive wall of water. They are sent to the Bridge one final time where they meet Agent Jones. Returning once more, all Loopers arrive in the Battle Bus above the Wall of Water.
Chapter 2: Season 3

Chapter 2: Season 3[]

The Flood[]

The giant Wall of Water created by The Storm collapsed, flooding the entire Island. The headquarters of SHADOW are now The Authority, a larger version of The Agency re-constructed by Jules, and implemented a large anti-flood wall that surrounds the compound. Midas was attacked by a Loot Shark.

Due to the flooding of the island, The Grotto & The Rig are no longer fit for use as a bases for SHADOW and are therefore abandoned. All former SHADOW bases and safehouses, save for The Authority, have also become defunct. Brutus also defects to GHOST,[6] initiating bad blood between him and Jules.[7] The Box Factory, under Meowscles' jurisdiction, has turned into Catty Corner under joint control with his son, Kit.

SHADOW continues the war against GHOST, who built The Fortilla as their new headquarters GHOST's operatives, such as Ocean, refused to give up the fight against SHADOW, and tried to make a comeback after they lost The Agency, but the war is overall less aggressive.

Rift Traveler[]

A Spaceship crash lands near the island. Loopers help its pilot to repair the spaceship and send it back through a Rift.


Nexus War: Thor[]

Back during The Blackout era, in Marvel Universe Reality: 616, Thor and Galactus were on a mission to stop The Black Winter from consuming their reality, allowing Galactus to consume the "5 special planets" to gain the power to defeat it. After consuming the first planet, Thor and Galactus came across a Sideways Rift that led to Reality Zero. Galactus could sense The Zero Point's energy inside, transforming Athena to Apollo and Artemis, and dove after it. Thor instructed him not to, but could not stop Galactus. He dove in before Galactus to warn the inhabitants, only to be met by a group of hostile Loopers. Before he could completely succumb to the effects of the Loop, he called out to Sif, the current guardian of the Bifrost, for help.

Chapter 2: Season 4

Chapter 2: Season 4[]

Earth's Mightiest Heroes[]

Lady Sif brought Tony Stark, Wolverine, Jennifer Walters, Mystique, Doctor Doom, Groot, Storm and many other Reality: 616 inhabitants to the Island through the Bifrost to help defeat Galactus. She also brought Helicarrier 64, which is used as a base of operations, along with S.H.I.E.L.D Quinjets. Tony Stark sets up a makeshift base in a barn and builds the Rift Beacons. Doctor Doom takes over Pleasant Park and turns it into Doom's Domain, and a group Sentinels decommissioned by Wolverine appear on two mountains.

Return to the Shadows[]

Both The Authority and The Fortilla have been completely abandoned, and the war between GHOST and SHADOW seems to have ended, with many members now working for themselves[8], waiting to return online[9] again., pursuing other goals. The last remaining SHADOW Henchman spends much of the season parading about with a newfound friend, an equally lone GHOST Henchman- becoming the Henchbros.

Stark Industries Arrival[]

With his beacons, Iron Man brought the entirety of Stark Industries to the Island, along with the surrounding Upstate New York, in order to have the tools to work on a plan to defeat Galactus. Dangerously approaching Reality Zero, Galactus deploys his Gorgers in order to scout the island and prepare his "meal" before his arrival.

Midas' Revenge[]

During Fortnitemares 2020, Midas returned, seeking revenge for his previous failures. To achieve this, he performed a dark ritual to gain the power of The Shadow Realm. This ritual involved his chair and a circle of candles. With his newfound power, Midas, now called Shadow Midas, attempted to take back control of the Island in the Midas' Revenge event. He appeared as a boss in the Ruins of the Authority with his own Shadowy Drum Gun. Shadow Midas reawakened deceased Ghost Henchmen and successfully managed to "hack" the Reboot system and Loop to get rid of Reboot Cards. He made it so that when Loopers were eliminated, they returned once more as a Shadow, creating his own army.


The Plan[]

Tony Stark creates a plan by modifying several battle buses with stark equipment, and then filling them with gamma bombs, then uses The Loop to make a billion modified battle bus clones. Over the course of the season, Galactus gets closer and closer, eventually being distincly visible in the night sky. After fading from view, Galactus arrives on the island through Artemis and rises from the ocean into Apollo.

The Devourer of Worlds[]

“
We need to get Galactus to eat as many buses as possible before he's done with the Zero Point.
”
— Tony Stark

Galactus finally commences into action, and fully rises. Telling the inhabitants to beware because he was hungry, The Devourer of Worlds destroyed Helicarrier 64, plunging the gigantic aircraft into the ocean, before ripping out the Zero Point from The Bridge using energy beams emitted from his hands, and attempts to absorb it. The Zero Point seemingly sensed its power being leeched on by something surrounding it, and cracked itself, trying to become a black hole again, like during The End and sucking Galactus and everything else. However Stark's intervention prevented this, and instead everyone were sent to The In-Between where countless battle buses were waiting for the battle.

The fight commences, and as The Zero Point is nearly totally exhausted by Galactus, he is tricked into consuming all of the battle buses, which detonate with the gamma cells and injure Galactus from the inside, sending him and a Zero Shard through a rift and back to Reality: 616.

Chapter 2: Season 5

Chapter 2: Season 5[]

Jones' Mission[]

“
I just need to stabilize the Zero Point and reseal the Bridge, but not let anyone escape. Easy, right?
”
— Agent Jones

During The Devourer of Worlds at Apollo, The Bridge gets heavily damaged due to The Zero Point being ripped out of the facility. Agent Jones wakes up in his Office with a call from Doctor Slone. She orders him to stop anyone escaping the loop, and to not attract the attention of The Seven. He would not hear the latter instruction, as foreshadowing for Chapter 2: Season 6, to not draw the attention of The Seven. He packs his Field Pack, and uses the Bridge Terminal to locate the best hunters across all realities.

A Warped World[]

The Zero Point had stabilized back from a black hole before a catastrophic event could have destroyed the island once again, and presently levitated above the center of the island. However, despite averting disaster, the area surrounding The Zero Point was warped and turned into a desert, with scattered, solid pieces of The Zero Point called Zero Point Crystals lying around. Elsewhere, around the island, locations and objects from past seasons are brought to the island, including Flush Factory, and Tilted Towers (although the latter was merged with Salty Springs to become Salty Towers). Oddly enough, these brought-back locations were in approximately the same coordinates as they were on the Chapter 1 island.

The IO Arrives[]

The Imagined Order quickly settles themselves throughout the island, still having access to The Bridge through their Surface Hubs. They build structures for the first 5 Hunters Jones brought at Hunter's Haven and Colossal Coliseum. The Predator follows Jones to Reality Zero and moves to Hunter's Haven, but makes Stealthy Stronghold his hunting ground.

Zero Point in Danger[]

“
I've dedicated my life to the Order. I've given everything. And for what? To just sit back and watch reality end? That's not who we are. At least it's not who I am. Not anymore.
”
— Reality Log 474

During his mission, Jones visits countless Realities to bring 30 Hunters to Apollo. His aim is to cause chaos within the Loop while the IO tries to fix The Zero Point. However, the IO failed to keep the Zero Point under control and Jones decided to take matters on his own hands.

Chapter 2: Season 6

Chapter 2: Season 6[]

“
What did you do to the Zero Point?
”
— The Foundation

Zero Crisis Finale[]

With The Zero Point reaching it's final destabilization stage, Jones realized that the Order isn't doing anything about it, and takes matters into his own hands. After his access to "Restricted File 8752" within IO was revoked, he goes rogue and flies to Apollo, ignoring Doctor Slone's orders. He makes his way through the chaos of the Loop, eventually getting close enough to toss his Portal Device into it. A few moments later, a meteor appears out of a rift in the sky, crashing into the sand near Dusted Depot. The meteor cracks, revealing another pod like The Visitor's and The Scientist's. The Foundation steps out of it, immediately attacking Jones. Jones stops the fight by offering to get him Geno and The Sisters in exchange for the Foundation's help in stabilizing the Zero Point. The Foundation agrees, attempting to untangle the Zero Point tendrils while Jones and The Looper close the new Pure Portals it creates. The Foundation is unable to untangle it, and creates The Spire to contain the Zero Point's blast, sacrificing himself by sealing himself inside The Zero Point. For it to work, Jones overloads his Portal Device and throws it inside, forcing himself to become trapped in The Loop. The Spire safely contains The Zero Point inside it, allowing it to heal and realign, while at the same time cutting off The Zero Point from The Imagined Order. A Reality Wave is released, forcing the island to go wild, with the center converted from a desert into an autumn-themed biome. Animals arrive and parts of the island change, being marked by autumn zones. Throughout Season 6, The IO vanish from Reality Zero, leaving behind some IO guards to protect The Bridge from intruders.

The Spire Quests[]

“
The longer Raz has the Spire Artifact, the more danger we're in. You can't wield that much power without attracting a lot of unwanted attention.
”
— Rebirth Raven

After Zero Crisis, The Looper decided to continue helping Jones and The Foundation, they began to hunt for anomalies.[10] Later, they were contacted by Tarana[11] who found a message from Jones for them asking to "Listen to the Crystal Song". Since the recording concerns The Spire, Raz decides to steal it and help The Looper in order to serve his interests. He helped The Looper infiltrate The Spire in order to decipher the message of the crystals. This message comes from The Foundation and asks The Looper to find Jones.

Both Raz and The Looper searched for Jones, talking to several of his Snapshots to find the Jones that The Looper encountered during Zero Crisis Finale. Some of them revealed some secrets about The Loop notably about the lose of memory and the snapshot phenomenon as well as their mission when coming here, but others warned that Jones was doomed and that they could only get help from "The First Jones". Once finding Jonesy The First, the later note that Jones asked the right person for help, speaking about The Looper. However, he wanted to test The Looper's strength first. After The Looper won the duel, Jonesy The First explained the truth behind The Spire. Saying that it will make people powerful, but that it will corrupt their minds. He told them to not let anyone powerful be corrupted by The Spire or Jones wouldn't stand a chance and to hurry to The Spire before it get more attentions from something or someone unspecified.

While The Looper was warned by Jonesy The First, Raz steals a fragment of the Spire's core and becomes Glyph Master Raz. Rebirth Raven, Lara Croft, and Jonesy the First warn that Raz's Power is attracting "unwanted attention" and ask The Looper to stop him and take The Spire artifact from him. They defeat Glyph Master Raz and collect The Spire Artifact, but Jonesy The First says that it's not the end.

Batman/Fortnite: Zero Point[]

In Reality: NF-1935, The IO teamed up with Lex Luthor to manipulate Batman into helping them regain control of the Zero Point after it had been sealed off by The Spire. They open a Sideways Rift in Gotham City and kick Batman into The Loop. After countless loop of battle royale, Batman finally escape the Loop and is able to explore the island, He teams up with a group of escaped Loopers, and then breaks into the IO Hatch. Inside The Bridge he finds the root of the Spire containing The Zero Point. Eternal Voyager jumps into the uncalibrated Zero Point, and is torn to shreds. Batman sees this, and understands they need to calibrate the Zero Point before entering it. Using everyone's individual "dimensional energy," they send everyone home one by one. Deathstroke sabotaged Batman and Catwoman's escape attempt, trapping them on the bridge. With just the two of them, and the equipment destroyed, they did not have enough dimensional energy to return to their reality. They are forced to travel back into the Loop using the Portal Device they stole from deathstroke to find Harley Quinn, so they can use her added dimensional energy to return. They are successful, and return to their world. While this is happening, Doctor Slone holds a meeting with Lex Luthor, revealing that Slone struck a deal with him to have Batman enter and escape the loop, in exchange for opening a Sideways Rift at the Daily Planet, although this may not be the full extent of their deal.

Hot Saucers[]

“
I've been seeing UFOs in the sky for months.
”
— Mari

At an unspecified time before The Last Reality's attack began, The Looper finally managed to escape The Loop.[12][13] They investigated damaged telescopes at the request of Bunker Jonesy, who suspected that people didn't wanted Islanders to watch the sky.

Bunker Jonesy later recontacted The Looper, about radio starting to ask him question, turns out to be someone named Mari, who used an authorized channel for her radio broadcast, in order to investigate the signs about aliens. Several interveners such as Turk from E.G.O and Dennis also intervened. However, Mari attracted the attention of the IO who ordered her to stop. However after her refusal, she was forced into silence by the IO during an altercation that everyone could hear on the radio, worrying Turk. Mari later returned, but announced she would put a pause to all of this. Around the same time, The Looper received a mission order whose sponsor was unknown. They were asked to terminate all unauthorized broadcast. During their mission, they found strange TV, with a logo, the same as that of the corn circle of Colossal Crops, worrying the sponsor of the mission.

Chapter 2: Season 7

Chapter 2: Season 7[]

Invasion[]

The Last Reality arrives with one of their Mothership and destroy The Spire in their attempt to absorb The Zero Point. They failed as it had moved down into The Bridge, deploying Abductors and Saucers in a full-out invasion against Reality Zero.

The IO finally fully reveal themselves to the Loopers to protect Apollo against the Alien Invaders. They set up Satellite Stations and Camps all around the Island to investigate the alien activities and The Mothership's movement. Slone recruits The Looper[14] and sends them to missions to find out more about the aliens.

There are rumors of a mole working for the Imagined Order, and Slone tasks the Looper with finding them.[15] Numerous IO agents; including Rick Sanchez, Maven, Rook and Marigold instruct the Looper in their recruitment to the IO. Slone's second task for the Looper has them infiltrate Believer Beach to earn the ringleader's trust.[16] They meet Joey, who knows how to disrupt the mothership's communications[17] by placing alien light communication devices on mountain tops.

In an attempt to gain information on the Island, The Last Reality creates the Rift Tour, a concert featuring Ariana Grande. They send a Party UFO to The Aftermath to begin the concert. The Loopers go through a memory tunell as The Aliens scan their brains before getting sent to the concert area. After all the songs are played, The Loopers are sent back to Apollo, unaware of the information given to The Last Reality.

Slone wants to investigate Sunny and find out if she is the ringleader of the anti-human sentiment on the Island.[18] The Looper goes to earn her trust by making contact with aliens[19] and help her welcome them.[20] Slone wants Hayseed, one of the island's biggest preppers, on the IO's side.[21] To lure aliens, the IO invents Inflate-A-Bulls. Seeing its effectiveness, Slone initiates Operation Fauxvine to handle the Saucers and the abduction crisis.[22]

Rook tasks the Looper to hunt down the traitor Riot, alongside Special Forces' help.[23] After searching the southeast corner of the Island, they eliminate Riot and report to command.[24] A major IO security breach happens and Slone wants to investigate. The Looper finds an access card for her and plant wiretraps to listen to "off the record" IO chatter, confirming the existence of a mole.[25] The mole leaks secret information about the IO to the public[26], and the Looper mindwipes the civillains. An existence of an "infiltratior of alien variety" is also found by Slone and the Looper goes to confront and eliminate them.[27] To learn more about the aliens, Slone and the Looper start monitoring their behavior and presence on the Island.[28] Noticing the rise in abduction numbers[29] and the aliens' interest in Slurpy Swamp, the Looper clears the area so the IO can investigate.[30] Desperate for an insider asset, Slone needs to squeeze intel from Joey or even recruit them.[31] The Looper bribes her with cat food and human gifts, and she agrees to try to discourage the aliens from annihiliating the Island.[32]

The IO comes up with a counter attack, but it has been compromized. Slone and the Looper decide to create a backup plan.[33] They collect Alien Devices to activate the Countermeasure Device at Corny Complex and create hundreds of bombs. Marigold helps the Looper to bait the aliens into abducting their bombs and pressure them into making a big move.[34] She "leaks" the location of their secret base to tempt the Mole into sharing it with the aliens,[35] but share it with locals by herself for insurance.[36] The countermeasure devices are prepared to strike the Motership, but the area it is expected to crash on is populated.[37] The Looper warns everyone to give them time to leave and prevent them from interfering.[38] The Mole activates the devices early but their plan is quickly spoiled by the Looper.[39] Identifying Maven as the Mole, the Looper is sent to eliminate her and does so successfully.[40]


Batman/Fortnite: Foundation[]

Just after the assault at the Spire, The Foundation wakes up at Reality: NF-1935, at the Gotham City harbor. He is greeted by Batman, and tries to move past him but is stopped by his grappling hook. They fight for a brief moment, as Batman thought the Foundation was a member of the Imagined Order, but is stopped when Foundation is surprised by Batman's knowledge of the Zero Point, allowing Batman to conclude Foundation was not part of the organization that trapped him in the Loop.

The Foundation explains the Seven's goal, and agrees to help Batman close the Sideways Rift. The Foundation is tricked by The Batman Who Laughs who dives after the rift. The Foundation follows him into the Loop, resulting in a quick skirmish between them. During the fight The Batman Who Laughs damaged the Foundation's visor, making him susceptible to the Loop's effects. He kills The Batman Who Laughs, but he begins to panic as his visor won't be repaired before the Loop resets. The Storm closes around him, but vanishes in an instant. He had escaped the Loop once more by being the last organic sentient entity in the center of the Storm as it closed.

The Batman Who Laughs is brought back by the Loop, now trapped inside of it. His visor, being crafted from the World Forge, protects him from the Loop's speech impeding effect. It does not however protect him from Snapshots, which are created soon after.

Operation: Sky Fire[]

“
This... is impossible. We thought it was gone for good!
Last time it nearly destroy the island!
”
— Doctor Slone

On the last day of Corny Complex's Abduction, Slone prepares a group of Loopers to infiltrate The Mothership to activate the bombs placed attached to the chunks of land being abducted. The Mothership starts the abduction, and all the Loopers are placed inside experiment holding cells. Slone hacks the Alien Jetpack to gain remote access into the Motherships systems, and opens the holding cells. They go through a number of rooms and tunnels, until they are spotted and cornered in the Abduction Chamber Window. During the first attempt to arm the bombs, some sort of countermeasure is activated and the activation fails. The windows close and a Corruption Cube rises from the floor, zapping Loopers to eliminate them. Slone weaponizes the Jetpacks and deactivates the Cube with the few remaining Loopers. The windows open again, and Slone attempts to activate them for a second time. It works, and Slone reveals that she cannot bring the Loopers back to the Island, as to not risk the cube returning as well.

The Loopers Reboot the Cube by placing their hands on it together, and The Cube activates an elevator. The Loopers are taken to a massive chamber full of Cubes and Abductors, with the Golden Cube being present at the very back of the room. The Mothership blows up, sending Loopers and everyone inside The Mothership flying down to Apollo. Multiple Abductors crash down to the Island, alongside a group of Corruption Cubes, the Golden Cube and the Rebooted Cube. The Looper gets hit by an Abductor, passing out.

Chapter 2: Season 8

Chapter 2: Season 8[]

Island, Corrupted[]

After the Cubes landed on the Island, they corrupted the landscape around, except the Golden Cube that landed near Holly Hedges. Slone and a group of IO Guards form a Convoy near Destroyed Dish, and collect remaining Alien technology. The Golden Cube quickly awakes, and begins rolling around the Island, visiting the each Corruption Cube and activating them one by one.

Betrayal Survivor[]

In the meantime, The Looper, who survived The Mothership crash was contacted by J.B. Chimpanski, who gave them information on what was going on and asked their help for the War Effort. Although still an IO agent, The Looper is clearly no longer loyal to the organization since they eliminated IO Guards under Charlotte's request and revealed information about the IO Outposts and IO Convoy to her.

Plan of the Six Sided beings[]

All of the Cubes, once activated, gave birth to new cubes, with the help of one of their ally: The Storm. However, Kevin's counterparts from an alternate timeline and finally broke out of Steamy Stacks was unable to do so. Joining its siblings in their invasion, Kevin began to move towards The Aftermath alongside them, leaving a trail of corrupted land in their wake.

Wrath of the Cube Queen[]

“
There is no escaping The Last Reality.
”
— The Cube Queen

All The Cubes conjoin to form a caste-like structure called The Convergence. The Cube Queen leaves her Golden Cube and begins to hover over the structure. The Convergence grows larger every day, eventually becoming The Pyramid. With the formation of The Pyramid, the Cube Queen releases her strongest Cube Monster: Caretakers.

Hope Not Lost[]

J.B. Chimpanski founds the War Effort to collect funds for Weapons to use against The Cube Queen. He starts with the funding of Turret Stations, before moving on to armmaments such as the Shockwave Launcher and Legacy Combat Assault Rifle. Later on he uses the funds to unvault the Combat Shotgun and Flint-Knock Pistol. In a final funding campaign, he brings two Salvaged B.R.U.T.E.s to the fight. One at Corny Crops, and the other at Pleasant Park. As a base, the Guava Fort is constructed to protect them and prepare an attack. Slone and her Convoy reach **REDACTED**, opening it and finally revealing the IO Staging Facility.

The End - Chapter 2 Finale
[]

“
Destroy them, my children.
”
— The Cube Queen

The Rebooted Cube teleports to the Guava fort to assist The Loopers against the The Cube Queen, as her onslaught was about to begin. She gathers her power to open Reality Zero's barrier for The Last Reality, with the Cube's Cradle in view. The Golden Motherships send out Saucers that attack The Loopers, but The Rebooted Cube protects them with it's shield. Golden Abductors start destroying Locations across the Island, with one targeting the Rebooted Cube, leaving The Loopers defenseless. Meanwhile in The Bridge, Slone and Gunnar being interrogating Jones, until The Foundation breaks in to save him. They run to the Zero Point chamber, and the Foundation tears down one of the panels as cover. The Foundation tells Jones to disable the Gyro System, and the Island begins to flip.

The Visitor and The Scientist arrive at Guava Fort, rescuing The Loopers. The Scientist takes them inside The Bridge through a Hatch. They run through the bridge as the Island rotates more and more, eventually reaching the Zero Point chamber to meet up with Jones and The Foundation. A Caretaker slams down on the glass ceiling, cracking it. The Caretaker slams down two more times, breaking it and flooding the room. The Looper floats up into the ocean, witnessing Apollo flipping over to Artemis. The Cube Queen comes in contact with water, and is defeated before rifting away. The portal closes, stopping the invasion of The Last Reality. The Island fully flips, causing a massive tidal wave that knocks the Looper out, beginning the Drift To Artemis.

Chapter 3: Season 1

Chapter 3: Season 1[]

“
I need your help to destroy the Imagined Order.
”
— The Foundation

The Flipside[]

The Looper wakes up at Looper Landing, finding the Foundation and a few other Loopers gathered around a campfire. The Foundation explains that with the IO exploiting the Zero Point, they will never be safe from threats such as The Last Reality, and the whole Omniverse is in danger as long as they are in control. He asks for the Loopers' help, explaining that if they succeed, they can all go home.

Seismic Activities[]

The IO quickly start drilling onto Artemis, and reach the surface through 5 Mole Teams. They take a cavern inside a mountain and convert it into Covert Cavern a stronghold for the IO. The Big Drill begins drilling in the direction of Sanctuary, the home of the seven, leaving sinkholes in its wake. IO Patrol cars also drive around the Island, attacking anyone they come across.


Without You[]

“
Hey, uh, Paradigm. Uh, it will not surprise you to learn that you remain public enemy number one for The Seven.
”
— The Scientist

The Looper secretly listens to the Seven Secure Broadcast Channel, learning that The Scientist is attempting to get the help of The Paradigm by having her return to the Island. The Scientist learns of the Looper listening in, and welcomes it. He warns them that war is coming, and to be ready for when it arrives.

The Seven launch various rockets for recon missions, with some being sabotaged by the Imagined Order. The Scientist employs the help of the Looper for assistance in stopping the sabotages.

Chapter 3: Season 2

Chapter 3: Season 2[]

New Characters: The Origin, The Imagined, Doctor Strange, Huntmaster Saber

The War[]

“
The Imagined Order has blocked building. And we're gonna get it back together.
”
— The Imagined

The Imagined Order finally unleash their attack on The Seven and Artemis, as a giant drill emerges from underneath Artemis, proven to be the cause of the previous earthquakes plaguing the island. Titan Tanks and Airships are deployed against The Loopers and The Seven, who clash with the invaders. The IO proceed to disable the building features, and overrun the defenders, who are subsequently saved by Doctor Strange, who opens a portal and reveals Iron Man, The Visitor, The Scientist, as well as Black Widow and two never-before seen members of the seven called The Origin and The Imagined, with the latter being one of the sisters.

Resistance[]

The Imagined recruits The Looper to help her bring Building back by destroying IO's build jammers. The Origin, Jones and The Imagined task the Looper to help them make a counterattack against the IO, and gain advantage in war.

The Seven Reclaim[]

The Seven's Resistance fight back against the Imagined Order to reclaim The Daily Bugle, Condo Canyon, Coney Crossroads, Rocky Reels and Tilted Towers from IO's control. The IO send Airship, Huntmaster Saber and IO Hunters to fight back but all of their effort soon turned out futile. All IO Outposts are destroyed and the IO forces soon retreat back to the center of the island.

The Collider[]

“
We have very credible intel that one of the IO's doomsday devices is now in production.
”
— Jones

Agent Jones receives intel that the IO is producing a doomsday device. IO's latest movement were spotted near Dispatch Depot. weeks later. The Paradigm detected golden energy fluctuations in multiple spots around Loot Lake. Soon the Imagined Order built The Collider, a device capable of detonating The Zero Point and destroy everything in The Loop.

Fortnite X Marvel: Zero War #1-4[]

The Visitor comes up with a plan to stop The Collider by using The Paradigm's Mecha Team Leader that was left on The Ice Moon. The Scientist suggests that going to The Ice Moon would be risky, but Spider-Man has the idea to call heroes from his reality to help them, specifically Thor and Storm with their abilities to manipulate the weather. The Origin tasks the group heading to Reality: 616 to retrieve The Zero Shard. Everyone agrees and splits up.

The Foundation, Jones, The Imagined and Spider-Man stealthily infiltrate The Bridge. Jones guides everyone through The Bridge, until he is distracted when he finds his office, and takes a picture of his late family with them. When they reach The Zero Point, they are stopped by Doctor Slone and Gunnar. Jones opens a Zero Point portal to Reality: 616, and he, Spider-Man, and The Imagined enter. On Slone's command, Gunnar strikes The Foundation through the ceiling. With her enemies out of the picture, Slone ignores the others with the little time they have left.

Spider-Man, Jones, and The Imagined arrive in Reality: 616, and they quickly send the Avengers to the Island to help the resistance. They head to fetch Wolverine to help track the The Zero Shard. Meanwhile, Doctor Doom is chosen by the inner circle of the Imagined Order to join them as a valued addition and Geno is able to recruit him.

The Imagined is able to track The Zero Shard at Mena Ngai, Wakanda. After a battle against the Wakandans, the princess of Wakanda, Shuri, quickly dispatches the fight and welcomes the group into Wakanda. Although The Imagined's tech is not able to trace down the exact location of the Zero Shard due to Wakanda's Vibranium interfering, Wolverine's scent determines that The Zero Shard was no longer there, and the smell of lizards remained. When Shuri leads the group to Serpent Valley, they discover a base guarded by Hybrids. As the group battles the lizard ninjas, the base is destroyed and they lose the trail for the Zero Shard. Spider-Man suggests heading to the Savage Land to find Stegron the Dinosaur Man, who is most likely responsible for taking the Zero Shard.

The Foundation, Scientist, Iron Man arrives on The Ice Moon safely with help of Thor and Storm's weather calming abilities. The group finds the broken Mecha Team Leader lying on the icy surface with The Paradigm alive in the mech. The Paradigm is awaken from stasis, and attempts to convince The Foundation that she is not a traitor, as her reason for siding with the IO during The Final Showdown was to prevent them from building a doomsday device that could destroy the entire island along with The Devourer. She goes on to explain that their plan to repair to the mech would not work unless they had a power source, unknowing of

Back in Earth 616, Spider-Man, Jones, The Imagined, Wolverine, and Shuri travel to the Savage Land, where they manage to find more Hybrids. After defeating them all, the group camp for the night. The Imagined explains her relationship to Geno to Wolverine as Jones explains his past with the Imagined Order to Spider-Man and how he worked with them to ensure his family's safety, but was never able to rest with them when they all passed and he remained immortal. The next day, Wolverine and Shuri tracked the Hybrids to a lab in the Savage Land, where they find Stegron and primal creations of the High Evolutionary using the power of The Zero Shard.

The group battles the High Evolutionary and Stegron as a distraction for Spider-Man to break into the lab, only to discover that leading the group to the lab and battling the High Evolutionary was Doctor Doom's plan to steal The Zero Shard himself using the Hybrids as his minions. As Doctor Doom uses the Zero Shard to escape, the battle against the High Evolutionary ends, as he only wanted to defend the Zero Shard. The High Evolutionary informs the group that Doctor Doom traveled to Reality Zero, and the group quickly use Jones' Portal Device to chase after him.

The group track Doctor Doom to The Ice Moon, where they find a castle trapping The Ice King and several of the powerful items he collected in ice. The Ice Moon proceeds to become colder despite Thor and Storm's efforts. Doctor Doom appears and battles them using The Zero Shard and many of the powerful items in the castle. Despite his efforts, Spider-Man is able to retrieve The Zero Shard and get it to the Mecha, fully repaired but requiring a power source. With The Zero Shard, the Mecha is repaired and Doctor Doom flees to the Island. The Mecha retrieves the group on the ground, destroys The Ice King's castle, and prepares to travel to the Island.

Collision / Fortnite X Marvel: Zero War #5[]

“
Today, we're gonna put an end to the Imagined Order. For good.
”
— The Paradigm
On The Ice Moon, The Origin gave the Loopers a mission briefing. The Paradigm then enters, and initiates Mecha launch sequence, with the Loopers entering the battle stations. The Mecha heads for the Island. It lands at Synapse Station, only to be greeted by battalions of the Imagined Order's tanks. The Mecha slowly walk head towards The Collider while fighting off the IO. The Mecha is hit by a barrage of missiles coming from IO Airships, heavily damaging it. Peely arrives in a Slurp truck and heal Mecha with it.

The Mecha pulls out its Light Blade, slashing through the remaining IO Airships. After the final IO vehicles have been destroyed, The Paradigm diverts all power to the Light Blade, and prepares to destroy the Collider. Before this can happen, an underground explosion occurs, and the Mecha falls into the ground below. The Paradigm and the Loopers are ejected from the Mecha.

The Zero Point has been exposed and the Collider is forcing it to explode. Jones comes to assist the Mecha crew. Doctor Slone arrives in a tank, and Jones begins leading the Loopers towards Slone, while the Paradigm attempts to get the Mecha back online. The Foundation arrives and eliminates several IO Guards. The Collider pulls The Zero Point towards the bottom of the Collider, shifting realities along the way.

Meanwhile, Geno joins the battle on the collider upper levels and fight against his daughters. Though Geno doesn't want to kill them, The Imagined sacrifices herself and falls into The Zero Point alongside Geno. Geno seemingly unharmed, angrily commands the IO to wipe these failures out of existence before entering the Zero Point.

The Paradigm manages to get the Mecha back online, and as Jones is distracting Slone with a speech, she crushes Slone and her tank with the Mecha's fist, destroying part of the bridge.

The Paradigm then begins to lift the Loopers, Jones, and The Foundation upwards towards the top of the Collider, with the latter two jumping into the Zero Point when it focuses on Geno. The Loopers then destroy the crystal detonators, and the Zero Point falls back below. The Paradigm protects the Loopers as the Collider collapses.

Meanwhile, Jones and the Foundation fist bump as they enter Geno's reality. They get to see Geno and The Imagined slowly discorporate, getting scattered across The Omniverse. Geno's remants travel through multiple Realities, winding up at a singular spot and reforming Geno.


Chapter 3: Season 3

Chapter 3: Season 3[]

“
I'm beginning to think that maybe freeing the Zero Point was a mistake.
”
— The Scientist

Vibin'[]

After The defeat of The Imagined Order with the destruction of The Collider, an island-wide celebration of The Seven's victory begins. Former IO facilities are vandalized, with with the former IO headquarters, Command Cavern, being transformed into Rave Cave. The Zero Point is now exposed and underneath The Glow, surrounded by debris from The Collider. It forms The Reality Tree, which then creates the Reality Falls biome on the island. A Klombo can be found "dead" near Loot Lake, but Haven reports Klombo footprints indicating that the species is still alive.

Reality Bloom[]

The Reality Tree grows its roots towards Logjam Lumberyard, creating a small tree from its roots which later grew into a Reality Bloom. This turned Logjam Lumberyard into Logjam Lotus, a location that changes its theme with each Loop. Roots also grew towards Butter Barn, Tumbledown Temple. It also changed other locations such as Sleepy Shrubs, Condo Canyon, and even brought back Lazy Lagoon, replacing The Daily Bugle.

Everything's My Fault[]

“
Those Zero Point energy shockwaves were flares! A galactic distress signal!
”
— The Paradigm

The Zero Point started bringing in a lot of powerful artifacts from many realities in an attempts to warn everyone on the island of the upcoming danger. The Paradigm received reports that some Loopers on the island and some members of The Seven randomly disappear.

Chapter 3: Season 4

Chapter 3: Season 4[]

Paradise[]

“
Stay safe. And listen, there will be no second chances here. The Nothing is--
”
— The Paradigm

The remaining members of the Seven attempted to investigate. As this is all happening, strange chrome substances are spreading across the island, consuming The Origin and The Scientist. The Paradigm escapes to Reality: 659. The Herald of the Last Reality arrives after watching over Reality Zero during of Chapter 3: Season 3 and is responsible for causing the chrome to spread. She sets up her Herald's Sanctum at the former Sanctuary.

Herald of the Nothing[]

“
We are searching for someone... and it is not you.
”
— The Nothing

To find a solution, Agent Jones contacts The Looper and briefly sums up the situation, he asks The Looper to reactivate AMIE. Jones then sends The Looper to the Reality Tree, The Looper picks up a message through the Roots and records it. AMIE decodes it and reveals it's a message from The Paradigm who warn the about The Nothing. The Looper and AMIE then decide to finish the Scientist's research on Chrome but nothing useful comes out of it. Jones then asks The Looper to find the Chrome Battle Plans that the Origin would have hidden, after finding them they decide to attack The Herald but defeating her was useless. After receiving a transmission from Paradigm, Jones, AMIE, and The Looper go dark. Meanwhile, Bytes was chosen by the Nothing itself to be warrior for The Last Reality.

Extinguishing Stars[]

Not only has Reality Zero been attacked, but it's not the only one. Sunbird an astronomer for the ages noticed that the stars, the distant realities of the Omniverse were extinguishing one after the other.

No Sweat's Policies[]

As Chrome spreads throughout Artemis No Sweat Insurance tries to save buildings from being affected by the Chrome. many buildings were lifted by No Sweat Insurance to become Cloudy Condos. Their headquarters at Tilted Towers were also lifted. As Chrome spreads and affects Coney Crossroads and Sleepy Sound, No Sweat Insurance saves the restauants from those locatons by lifting them off the ground.

An IO Airship over Rave Cave was taken by the Loopers and becomes The Flairship. It is being used to rescue residents of the island from Chrome spread as the airship flies around the island and stops at major locations.

Reality Zero will fall[]

A while after, Jones once again contacts The Looper and asks them to place signal boosters to receive the new transmissions from Paradigm who tells them that Reality will perish but she has a plan, she then tells them about Zero Point because she needs notes from The Scientist. After the notes were found Paradigm contacted The Looper in order to ask them to have Jones tell everything he knew about the Loop Breaker project. After all this is done Jones, AMIE, and The Looper search for a way to send all the collected information to The Paradigm and manage to do so with the help of Bushranger. After, Paradigm asks them to standby for the fateful moment.

Fracture - Chapter 3 Finale
[]

“
Home is gone.
”
— The Paradigm

A group of loopers on the Island build a stronghold on the top of the Reality Tree as a final defense against the power of The Herald. Several Chrome Vortexes appear, spreading the Chrome across the entire Island. A final Chrome Vortex forms near the Reality Tree, along with The Herald. Despite the efforts of the loopers to attempt to destroy The Herald, she forms a massive body made of Chrome and fuses with the Reality Tree, resulting in her sacrificing herself, but leading to the nearly complete destruction of both Artemis and Apollo, excluding the Zero Point.

The Looper finds themselves in space surrounded by several chunks of land remaining after the fracture of the Island. The Zero Point, however, manages to tether onto pieces of land, as well as the Looper, and pull them all in to begin the reconstruction of a new island. The loopers begin exploring the lands and completing tasks to collect Zero Fragments to restore enough power to the Zero Point to pull in more pieces of the island and eventually initiate Zero Fusion.
