The Zero Point begins to take pieces of land and characters from different realities, including Reality: 659 where The Paradigm was. With enough land, the Zero Point fuses all the land together, creating the new Island.

Chapter 4: Season 1

Chapter 4: Season 1[]

Oathbound Quests[]

“
Hmmm, it looks like every member of The Seven is currently missing...
”
— AMIE

Out of the pieces of land that were pulled in to fuse into the new Island, one of the locations was The Citadel, a medieval castle from another reality ruled by Oathbound. The Looper takes orders from AMIE as they discover more about the Island, as well as Oathbound. The kingdom's power source is a refined material mined from a quarry called Shattered Slabs: Kinetic Ore. When refined, the material becomes an extremely powerful energy source that is used in the fuel, weapons, and items of the Oathbound, a material never before seen on the Island before. Several Characters and locations on the Island have formed an alliance, swearing an oath that bounds them to the kingdom, becoming the earliest version of the nearly perfected Imagined Order. The kingdom is ruled by The Ageless, a young Snapshot of Geno.

Rift Gate[]

“
The test rift locked onto The Scientist. And something... something horrible is happening to him!
”
— AMIE

Rift Warden Stellan has been tasked by the Ageless Champion to create a Rift Gate, a bridge to travels realities. He then asks for The Looper help and later explain that the Rift Gate is Ageless Champion's dearest wish but also that he has his own reason to built it. After further help from The Looper, he reveals something he told to no one: the existence of a being, a "Shapeless Man" who always imagined a world of perfect order and explains that it's necessary to finish the Rift Gate or else there will be terrible consequences. AMIE's test subjects sent through test rifts encountered The Nothing sculpting The Scientist and the others into something new. The test subjects returned to AMIE corrupted with new forms. Operation Rescue the Scientist begins and AMIE is planning to use Stellan's Rift Gate to get to the Scientist.

Rift Sabotage[]

“
He does not care if opening the Gate rends the Island in two!
”
— Rift Warden Stellan

Stellan activates the Rift Gate using the unstable energy from the Kinetic Ore. but someone sabotaged the gate and manipulated the coordinates causing it to overload and explode. The gate is destroyed, the sky is torn and The Shapeless Man disappeared. Stellan finds a prophecy from exiled sages of thee Oathbound. The texts prophesize a... "a beautiful monster, enabled by the hubris of a champion." and wonders if silence itself is the prophecy. Stellan thanked us for assisting him in making of the Rift Gate and and bid farewell. Later, Evie received transmissions from the Peace Syndicate that said "It is time for us to come to you."

Chapter 4: Season 2

Chapter 4: Season 2[]

The season started with several futuristic buildings and small islands being rifted onto the island. Faulty Splits is crushed, and in its place MEGA City now stands tall, with a new biome forming on the landmass that got rifted in.

Syndicate Quests[]

“
Oh, and all of our intel on the Army of the Last Reality has been corrupted.
”
— Evie

The Looper is recruited into the Peace Syndicate and tasked to find the saboteur who's been trying to start a war between the syndicates by messing with the Peace Syndicate and pinning blame on other syndicates. While the Looper is on their way to check Peace Syndicate computers, they encounters a suspicious man named Triarch Nox near the computer. The man told the looper "I'd advise staying away from that terminal..." After checking the computer and find out that the files on Army of The Last Reality have been corrupted. Triarch Nox tries to take out The Looper, but fails. The Peace Syndicate unites with the River Guard, Fox Clan, The Luminaries and The Unseen to search for the saboteur. In the end, everyone becomes friends and the problem is solved.

The Rumbles[]

Large cracks and fractures originating from the impact of MEGA City have appeared across the island followed by multiple Zero Point Earthquakes. Biomes begins to split as the mysterious tree roots rises from the cracks.

Chapter 4: Season 3

Chapter 4: Season 3[]

Wilds[]

“
The power that once flowed through your island has faded, and formidable enemies have taken notice.
”
— Optimus Prime

The central part of the Island collapsed, revealing an ancient jungle below. The Zero Point is exposed but petrified and constantly groaning. Optimus Prime hacked the radio across the island to warn everyone that "This reality is doomed to collapse, but hope is not yet lost."

The Apparatus[]

“
I think you know that it takes a little more than dropping a tank on me to take me out.
”
— Slone

Doctor Slone is finally freed from the underground after the surface of Asteria collapsed. She then began to study the jungle and the ruins of The Ancients civilization that inhabited it. While locked up in the Jungle she recorded several audio logs of her findings about the ruins and the ancient civilization. She finds The Apparatus and plans to fix it up. Research in the Rumble Ruins reveals several murals, including one concerning Optimus Prime and another a mysterious silhouette. Optimus Prime later contacted Slone and explained the situation to her, while revealing the procedure to follow, he then left a message on the Rumble Ruins murals to tell everyone to study and find the secret hidden among the stars, to seek answers from the starless void. He later send other radio messages concerning the clues left by the Ancients to study the stars, before leaving them a final cryptic message "the key to your future lie in the past".

The Eclipse[]

“
It's not a natural cosmic phenomenon. And it's coming our way... with intent.
”
— Slone

Slone reveal to someone through her Audio Recorder that Optimus Prime told her a way to save everyone. After deciphering the glyphs, Slone realized that they predicted an Eclipse, but it didn't match the astrological data. However, her long-range cosmic scans revealed the presence of a signal, an unnatural cosmic event that she recognizes, which is coming our way with intent. She determined that the ancient people couldn't predict this eons ago, unless it had happened before. After the repair and installation of The Apparatus, it was revealed not to be a telescope, but a Stellar projector, which served as a countdown before the fateful arrival of the being responsible for the Eclipse. At the same time the murals of rumble ruins began to glow. Her Audio Recorders turns out to be addressed to Agent Jones, whom she reluctantly asks for help for a mission.

Chapter 4: Season 4

Chapter 4: Season 4[]

Last Resort[]

“
There's something in Kado's deepest, best-protected vault that Slone wants.
”
— Nolan Chance

The time traveling vampire and the wealthy leader of Eclipse, Kado Thorne finally arrived as the Glyphs and the Apparatus announced it. He made Island his home, draining the Island's wealth and treasures to fund opulent properties: Sanguine Suites, Relentless Retreat, and Eclipsed Estate. Meanwhile, Doctor Slone launches heist operations to steal Thorne's time machine whose existence Optimus had revealed to her from deep under Thorne's estate, this seems linked to her plan and the mission she plans to entrust to Jones.

Time Machine Heist[]

“
Kado's up to somethin suuuper sinister.
”
— Mae

Slone hires Nolan Chance to steal the Rift-powered Time Machine from Kado Thorne. With the help of The Looper, Nolan manages to recruit the members of his former Heist Crew for the heist: Antonia, Piper Pace, Fish Thicc and Mae as well as a new recruit: Khaby Lame. Jones also returned to the Island, reluctantly helping Slone with her plans.

While Thorne came out of the time machine after his journey back to "2020 03 25" to get Oro's head for his collection, the Heist Crew tried to rob his vault. But after discovering the intruders in his estate, he transformed into his Revenant Form and tried to kill them, forcing the crew to flee.

The Eclipse Begins[]

The Eclipse began with a permanent Super Blood Moon and Solar Eclipses caused by Thorne's psionics powers, as predicted by The Apparatus. Around this time, Shimmerdusk arrives on the Island to continue her hunt of Kado Thorne.

After the Heist Crew's failure to steal the time machine, Kado Thorne remained in his Revenant form to protect his it himself as the vaults doors were no longer functional. He also established his next target, "2018 07 12" the period of Season 5, when Athena Island undergoes the World Collide. However his plan appeared not to be about obtaining items for his collection, but something else as yet unrevealed.

Later, the Heist Crew managed to steal his machine, and take it to their base.

With the machine stolen, Shimmerdusk was free to make her move on Thorne. Throughout the Fortnitemares 2023 Quests, she enlists the help of The Looper to prepare. She has them stock up on ammo, kill Thorne's Guards, smash his valuables, etc etc. After weeks of preparing, she decides that they are ready and they go to confront the vampire, to eliminate him, which was successful. With Thorne dead, she declares: "It is done. This revenant will no longer prey across time and space."

“
See you on the other side of all this, buddy.
”
— Jones

With the machine in Slone's hands, they begin testing it, bringing objects from "2018 07 12" to the present. Once it is fully tested and ready for use, Slone sends Jones into the past for a mission. Indeed, since the Zero Point of the present was unusable because it was petrified, Optimus had guided Slone to send someone in the past, in order to use the Blackhole which had previously created Apollo and Artemis and to modify it for this time to generate a Big Bang in order to create new Realities, without changing the future. This would allow the inhabitants of Asteria to escape before Asteria collapse.

Chapter 4: Season OG

Chapter 4: Season OG[]

Worlds Collide[]

“
Everything you do will ripple forward to this very moment...
”
— Slone

Jones time travels back to the Chapter 1: Season 5 for his extraction mission: allowing inhabitant of Reality Zero to escape from Asteria by creating new Realities where they could take refuge by acting on The Black Hole. Jones and his Time Machine landed in Pleasant Park. However, this isn't Reality Zero's past, but an alternate timeline, as several things are different:

  • Some characters don't exist and their roles are filled by others or their fate is significantly altered from what it was for their Reality Zero counterparts.
  • The Sideways Rift closed earlier than expected, even before The Cube appeared.
  • No Sweat Insurance building replaced Tilted Towers' Castle Tower, and even depicts events that have not yet happened when it should have appeared much later, during the period of Season 8 and be placed at the "unlucky spot", where the Sporting Goods shop is located.
  • Houses in Snobby Shores and Fatal Fields were also replaced even though this should never have happened.
  • Other small anachronisms are present, with the Moisty Palms sign, supposed to appear during the Season X period, as well as the Explorers book concerning the Chalice of Cubetastrophe located in Wailing Woods which is from Chapter 4: Season 3 period.

Darkness Rises[]

“
And it's not to change the future, by the way; drop that fantasy right now.
This is an extraction, and you're leading the way.
”
— Slone

During Chapter 1: Season 6 period of this new timeline, the changes caused the absence of The Butterfly Event as Kevin never appeared in the desert and never placed runes, it didn't have enough energy to attack the Island and therefore the Loopers never tried to destroy it. Kevin the Cube is now alive and floating above The Vault. While Jones gradually makes his way to Dusty Divot, but first has to recover his trailer that fell into Loot Lake after an accident, someone starts to prepare for the arrival of The Scientist by turning Dusty Divot's diner into a rocket construction site.

Chill and Treasure[]

“
This... is not my first message, is it? You must be making progress.
Or you're screwing everything up.
”
— Slone

For some unspecified reasons, a very reduced version of the Iceberg arrived on the Island. The absence of Polar Peak and all that hid caused the non existence of the war between The Ice King and The Prisoner as well as the battle against The Devourer from this timeline's story. Fate of Characters such as The Ice King, The Prisoner, Hybrids and The Devourer in this reality is therefore unknown. The Scallywags also arrived earlier and due to the various changes but they were unable to dock their ship to create Lazy Lagoon. They also established their Camps in different locations than in Reality Zero. While all of this happened, Kevin the Cube is now trying to extract The Zero Point out of the Vault, while construction of the Rocket is almost finished at Dusty Divot, with Jones arriving with the Time Machine.

Blast Off![]

“
I guess.. this is goodbye for this me and that you.
But we'll meet again.
”
— Slone

Due to the timeline's alterations, The Final Showdown never occurred, instead The Zero Point was extracted out of Loot Lake, pulled right below Kevin the Cube which was trying to absorb its energy which caused the Scientist's Meteor to return once again. As The Zero Point was never cracked or unstable, The Seven never created any Rift Beacons or Rift Zones. Instead, The Seven with the help of Jones quickly finishes The Rocket and tape the Time Machine on it as the final preparation for Jones' mission.

The Big Bang[]

The rocket begins to take off from Dusty Divot. The energy from the time machine begins to make the rocket malfunction and transform it into several random objects, however the rocket is able to focus its course to the frozen Meteor. Continuing the plan as it did during The End Event, with the time machine, six more rockets appear from the future to assist in creating a large Rift below the Meteor. The time machine's energy start to cause the Meteor to transform into several different objects, before it was successfully pushed into the large rift. Then, a Rift appears over Loot Lake which a Rocket emerges from, with the Meteor following behind it. All the Loopers are launched into the sky after the initial rocket hits the Zero Point, destabilizing it. The Meteor, guided by the six other rockets, hits the bubble which forms around the destabilized Zero Point. It struggles to break through, eventually crashing into the Zero Point, throwing players further away. The Black Hole then forms, which sucks all of matter into it and completely destroy this timeline from existence.

Suddenly, a Big Bang occurs, creating new galaxies, each holding new realities. As existence starts to form, The Looper witnesses the birth of these new galaxies while also going through a series of them, including a LEGO Galaxy, a Racing Galaxy, and a Musical Galaxy. In parallel, in the original timeline therefore in Reality Zero, The End also occurred, creating the Black hole then generating Apollo and Artemis. Jones returned to our time and e Asteria's inhabitant used the Battle Buses to travel to Helios.

  • Chapter 5
  • Chapter 6

Before Chapter 5: Season 1

Before Chapter 5: Season 1[]

Following the result of The Big Bang, several new Realities were created with one of them being Helios. Following an undefined period of time, several decades or centuries before the arrival of Asteria's inhabitants on Helios, an organization named The Society was founded by Montague, who presented his idea to the other elites of the Island during an inauguration in Grand Glacier. This led elites such as Nisha's and Valeria's family to join The Society.

Decades later and once they were born, Hope, Nisha, Valeria, and Kavel were raised in The Society. Nisha and Valeria later took the place of their parents as heads of their respective families and rose to the highest rank, joining Montague at the top of The Society. Oscar was also offered a place after having climbed the ranks when he was an outsider. Peter Griffin, after becoming quite strong thanks to Meowscles' expired Slurp, became rich and also joined The Society becoming one of the bosses under unknown circumstances. The Society gradually took control of the Island hiring goons and people like Killian and Clara to become their Operators, along with all other elites joining their cause, except Silas Hesk, who decided to stand against them despite the multiple invitations to join the organization from Montague.

However, Hope realizing how terrible they were, was driven by her sense of justice, rejected the ideals of The Society and left the organization before founding The Underground, a rebel organization dedicated to fighting The Society that Jones later joined. Seeking more power, Valeria would dig into her grandfather's old books on The Pantheon where she would read about the powers of Pandora's Box, she would share her findings with the other leaders leading them to search for Pandora's Box. Believing Jones had the answers, the Bosses kidnapped Peely.

Chapter 5: Season 1

Chapter 5: Season 1[]

Underground vs Society[]

Searching for his missing friend, Jones embarked on a mission of revenge against The Society, with the help of Hope. The Underground started an open war against The Society.

Takedown Quests[]

“
I hope Montague enjoys watching us tear his little Society apart.
”
— Jones

The Underground, led by Hope, continued their battle against The Society, led by Hope's sister Valeria. As time passed, the forces of The Society slowly faltered, and fought amongst themselves. With the help of The Looper, Jones managed to rescue Peely from the grasp of their enemies, ending his quest for vengeance. However Valeria continued her research about the box-shaped relic, seeking its power.

Odyssey Quests[]

“
There is a woman I thought might be a kindred spirit. Valeria. But she seeks power, not insight. Whatever the Mosaic is warning us about... I believe Valeria is responsible.
”
— Odyssey

As The Society's power continues to dwindle, a strange traveler arrives on the Island called Odyssey and a strange Mosaic that bears a warning was discovered. After hearing about The Looper and seeing part of their destiny, she recruited them to help her in her research about The Mosaic that she believe herald something and that it's linked to the ancient power that she feels on the Island. As The Looper manages to find more fragments of The Mosaic, Odyssey reveals that the Society Medallions that the leaders of The Society possessed contained a fragment of this power, and that this same power grows stronger.

On March 2nd 2024, after a series of earthquakes near Valeria's camp, a giant hand emerged from the ground holding the chained Pandora's Box. On March 3rd 2024 during the Pandora's Box Opening event, the box was broken free from the chains and unleashed a giant beam of light and countless souls out of the box.


Chapter 5: Season 2

Chapter 5: Season 2[]

Myths & Mortals Quests[]

“
So Zeus wants to end mortalkind.
”
— The Oracle

With Pandora's Box opened by Valeria, The Pantheon has arrived on the Island, with Zeus immediately declaring that all mortals shall die by his wrath. The Gods started to punish The Society for opening the box, with the bosses left barely alive, Montague and Valeria both escaped The Island, while Oscar and Nisha kept a low profile. However, some gods, such as Aphrodite and Poseidon, as well as immortals such as Medusa immediately disagree with Zeus' viewpoint on mortals and wish for them to thrive. All three of which notice Hope's potential and are interested by her. The Looper meets The Oracle, who envisions their fate, telling them they must prove the worth of humanity to Zeus and the guardians of Olympus before the skies turn black.

The Oracle then tested The Looper to see if they could be the Hero of Mortals. After having passed the tests of strength, agility and wisdom of The Oracle, The Looper was recognized as the Hero of Mortals whose mission is to unite immortals against Zeus. Then, they were sent to provoke Hades to challenge him. After passing Hades' test which consisted of defeating Cerberus to prove that they could be able to defeat Zeus, The Looper was challenged by Ares and declared war on the gods by defeating the latter and stealing his medallion.

Rise of Midas Quests[]

“
I don't fear these gods. No power is absolute.
”
— Ascendant Midas

Midas Rises: Marigold, suspecting that Midas is somewhere in The Underworld, gathers many of Midas' Old Crew back together and went to Helios. SHADOW, which has been under the leadership of Brutus since Midas' disappearance, takes over The Marigold Yacht and sets up SHADOW Briefings around the Island. Marigold recruits The Looper to investigate The Underworld but they end up learning The Golden King escaped by managing to have an underworld guard unshackle one of his chains, then using his golden touch to soften the cuffs and lock to his cell door. Midas then enchanted lava with his Golden Touch to bring vengeance to the gods. He reunites with his former associates and his daughter, meanwhile The Looper and Marigold study the power of the Chains of Hades.

Et tu, Brutus: After meeting The Looper, Midas wanted to check the loyalty of his former number 2: Brutus. He asked The Looper to check his loyalty by recruiting mercenaries and then checking to see if he was listening to Midas with bugs. The Looper found bugs and reported it to Midas, who then demanded to confront Brutus. Fortunately for Midas, these bugs were only those of Hope, dating from when The Marigold belonged to The Society and Midas was reassured to see that his old friend had not betrayed him.

Jules vs. The Golden Touch: Marigold asked The Looper to work with Jules on how the Chains of Hades worked to find a way to contain Midas' Golden Touch, together they manage to harvest and transform the essence of Styx which Jules managed to infuse into gauntlets that she forged, allowing Midas to control his Golden Touch.

The Great Marigold Yacht Heist: After he received the gauntlets, Midas revealed his story in the form of myths to The Looper, and with his crew, they began their plan to escape in a new location far from Helios, for his ambitions. To this end the members of the crew, Meowscles, Skye, TNTina, Brutus, and Marigold gave several missions to The Looper in order to act as bait so that Midas and the other can go ahead into Spawn Island, safely away from Hades while Brutus stays behind to prepare The Marigold yacht to pick them up and traverse on it to go on their new, fruitful adventures as reunited friends. Later the crew left The Island.

New Allies Against Zeus[]

Aphrodite's Quests: After being informed of the situation by Hades and Ares, Zeus became enraged and sent Edicts to the gods while also recruiting followers. At the same time, Aphrodite joins The Looper's side, although she thinks the fight is lost, but little by little she regains her fighting spirit thanks to the help of Poseidon. After finding and deciphering some of Zeus' Edicts, The Looper and The Oracle discover that Zeus plans to destroy The Looper, and with another that he plans to recruit Artemis, however The Looper manages to gain an audience with her and recruit her before Zeus.

Cerberus' Quests: Zeus orders Cerberus to dig up information on The Looper's actions, however he contacted The Looper to tell them he won't tell anything, if they help him for something. Indeed, prior to this, Hades entrusted an "artifact" (a ball) to Cerberus, but it was lost. After helping Cerberus, Hades finally contact The Looper to reveal them he took Cerberus' ball for safekeeping during Zeus' tempest. Realizing that it made him very agitated, he put the scent of Cerberus' ball on The Looper so that Cerberus could find and recover it. However, for the other heads to submit to The Looper, the latter had to defeat Cerberus once more. After that, Cerberus recovers his artifact and promises to not reveal anything about The Looper to Zeus. The Oracle then contacts The Looper, telling them to show Zeus' edict to Medusa, so that she would join them.

The Revolution of Olympus[]

“
The immortality of the Olympians is not absolute.
It has a source, and that source can always be destroyed.
”
— The Oracle

Hope restores her fighting spirit, and reforms The Underground to assist The Looper in trying to stop Zeus, angry that he has been abusing power and needs to be stopped.

Medusa's Quests: Medusa calls upon The Looper, wanting to take back her property that Zeus stole from her a long time ago. While searching, they find an hourglass that was created by Hephaestus. The Oracle tells The Looper that the Olympian's immortality isn't absolute and that the Stormforged Hourglass is Zeus's source, and that it can be destroyed. After destroying all the Crackling Hourglasses, Medusa then explains that they were crafted with Zeus's own godly essence to increase his power and by destroying them, it weakened the god. With Zeus's godly essence that was inside the hourglasses, she crafted a charm that she gave to The Looper to empower them, before sending them to destroy Zeus's Stormforged Hourglass in a raid of Mount Olympus along with all the immortal allies : Aphrodite, Artemis, Apollo, Poseidon, Perseus and Medusa as well as The Underground. This ended up making him mortal and then The Looper confronted the king of gods in a death match that the god ultimately lost. At the end of the battle against Zeus' faction, Hope tagged his former throne with the symbol of The Underground proclaiming a reign of freedom.

Darkened skies... Choking dust...[]

“
Storm clouds gather.
The Wanderer draws near.
”
— The Oracle

The Oracle reminds the Looper of the prophecy about their own fate, concerning darkened skies and choking dusts, remarking that The Wanderer approaches, and that they need to be prepared for war, but not without hope, because it will lead them through the darkness.

During the Sandstorm Event, Zeus used his Mount Olympus statue to overload Pandora's Box and unleash a Nitro sandstorm from it. Everything that happens is just as what was prophesied by The Oracle. The sandstorm contains a shark-themed boat housing enemies attracted by the power of Pandora's Box that is slowly approaching the Island.

Chapter 5: Season 3

Chapter 5: Season 3[]

“
(This) -Island is... fine. 3 points out of 5 if I'm honest. All this self-serious architecture! And that sad mountain monument to the banished Gods! Your only God now is ME, dear ones. And I will raze this entire pathetic island TO THE GROUND! Once my Leviathan awakes.
”
— Megalo Don's Mysterious Transmission

The Wasteland Warriors arrived on the Island along with the sandstorm and claimed ownership of the Wasteland Biome and all of the Nitro. Hope discovered that Megalo Don plans to destroy the Island, but won't be able to launch his raid until his Leviathan ship is refueled with enough Nitro. He therefore asks The Machinist, the Wasteland Warriors' engineer to extract Nitro to be able to power the Leviathan. In the meantime, Megalo Don and his crew cause chaos and destruction over the Island, leaving Hope, Jones, The Looper and their allies to try and stop him before it's too late.

Wrecked Quests[]

Welcome to The Wasteland[]

Hope and Jones discover that The Machinist is building a pipeline from Redline Rig to power The Leviathan back up, they quickly contact The Looper to fight on their behalf while they continue to gather information in order to find a way to takedown their leader Megalo Don. Hope makes a makeshift base at Sandy Steppes, she soon discovers a gem holder connected to Pandora's box. Hope then asks The Looper to place surveillance devices in the Wasteland, and later discovers rumors about a Wanderer. However she believes those are only rumors. Later, Hope and Jones contact The Looper and asked them to talk with Wastelanders Brite Raider and Rust. They tell them that Megalo Don has been writing his plans down, and that something has been going on at Redline Rig. The Looper is then tasked with raiding the rig and sending over information from the Rig's terminals to Hope, making them learn The Machinist is the sole competent engineer of the Wasteland Warriors.

Recovering Pandora's Gem[]

“
I had a feeling we hadn't seen the end of Pandora's Box.
And... I'm worried I'm right. The Wastelanders have been finding gems...
”
— Hope

A bit later, after hearing the chatter in the Wasteland, Hope discovers the Wastelanders have been finding Gems, that she suspect came from Pandora's Box. Realizing that Megalo Don and his crew are out there searching for these Pandora Gems as well, Hope and Jones sent The Looper to go collect the first gem at Brutal Beachhead and bring it back to them, The Looper finds and hands over the first gem to Hope, who begins to develop a strange headache whenever she's close to a Pandora gem.

Soon after, Hope tasks The Looper with retrieving another one of Pandora's gems this time at the Nitrodrome after impressing Ringmaster Scarr, she agrees to trade over her Pandora Gem. The Looper gives Hope the second gem as she begins to tell them about all the short visions she's been getting every time she interact with a gemstone, Hope tells The Looper to not tell Jones anything as she doesn't want him to get worried about her. Later Jones discovered the third gem was owned by Peabody. Once the Looper managed to get it after helping their auto technician in training, Dummy and gave the gem to Hope, she got another headache, stronger this time. She tells The Looper she's been getting visions of Megalo Don working together with The Wanderer.

Around the same time, the mysterious being began wandering through the Wasteland while Megalo Don reveals that he and The Wanderer share a common goal and that the latter has important matters to resolve. Megalo Don orders his comrades that nobody should see his face.

With the pipeline starting to make good progress, and the Warriors' plan nearing a successful ending, Hope orders to find the last gem. After talking to Peabody, The Looper learns Megalo Don keeps his valuables in caches on the Island. After searching the caches in vain, they tried to recover Megalo Don's map of his hidden cache, once done The Looper recovered the Gem and gave it to Hope which causes The Oracle, who prophesied The Wanderer, to return and assist The Underground in what comes next. After this, Megalo Don, furious that his gems had been stolen, demanded that the heads of Hope and Jones be brought back to him, while boasting that his plan would soon be in motion and that a wise friend had assured him that no one could stop him.


Leviathan's Folly[]

“

”
— The Wanderer

On August 10th, 2024 at 2:00 PM ET: The event started with an opening speech by Megalo Don giving an unhappy ending to everyone. Nitro begins fueling his Leviathan until the pipeline gets disrupted by The Wanderer and explodes. The explosions travel through the pipelines running through Megalo Depot and connecting to Redline Rig, causing them to explode and leading to a bigger explosion near the location, uncovering Pandora's Box inside the crater.

Following the event and Megalo Don's failed plan, The Oracle warns of a war that is coming, and that Hope must take a stand to fight The Wanderer who has taken Pandora's Box powers, claiming that she has everything she needs. With Magneto on Hope's side, he has already prepared beacons to call in reinforcements from his reality.

Chapter 5: Season 4

Chapter 5: Season 4[]

“
Mother... I am building my new kingdom. This place reeks of failure, of fault... of imperfection. But it will not be this way for long. I will set this place to order. I will make it perfect. By gentle, guiding hand... or by brutal fist of submission.[41]
”
— Doom

After Megalo Don's pipeline exploded revealing Pandora's Box, The Wanderer reveals himself as the great Doom and immediately absorbs all the powers of Pandora's Box before recruiting many dangerous villains to his side by summoning The Raft.

The Underground teams up with all the heroes summoned by Magneto, with Jones taking up the mantle of Captain America, while Hope prepares herself to become a hero. Nisha and Kavel, former members of The Society finally decided to join The Underground. With previous allies and members such as Starr Oakley, Brite Raider, Rufus and many more rejoining The Underground's forces in an effort to help their friends Hope and Jones.

Chapter 5: Season 4 Story Quests[]

Gwenpool was captured by Doom's forces and imprisoned in one of The Raft's cells. She asked help from The Looper to accomplish her mission, first by asking them to take out a guard and steal their ID Chip. After she analysed the chip, Gwenpool tells them that it doesn't have everything she needed. She therefore asks The Looper to hack into The Raft's systems for her by using Doom's passphrase, CYNTHIA. The Looper recovered the drive that they then gave to Gwenpool. Later, as part of the last step of her plan she asks The Looper to search around The Raft to recover computer chips that Shuri needs for her plan, involving The Rift Beacons. The Looper does so and gives her everything she needed, before leaving. Gwenpool would later escape her cell and reunite with the other heroes.

Later, The Looper and Jones try to find Doom together, but are unsuccessful. Hope calls The Looper to come investigate the crater left over by Pandora's Box next to Redline Rig as something weird was spotted. Upon arriving and investigating, The Looper finds the broken mask of Doom. After a discussion with The Oracle, they come to the conclusion it's a prototype, and hands it over to Hope for further analysis. After bringing the broken mask to Hope, she expresses her doubts on how to stop Doom. The Looper and Jones later continued their investigation at Redline Rig to see if there are other pieces of Doom's broken armor, with Jones noting that Doom must have wanted them to find his mask. However they don't find anything else. Afterwards, Hope sends The Looper to retrieve the recording devices she placed at Fractured Four and Cynthia von Doom Memorial. They find that Doom has been there pondering his thoughts, as he speaks about the corrupted powers of the Box and how it keeps trying to overtake him, leading him to go into hiding for now, to make himself new armor. But this information tells them nothing about his whereabouts. So Hope subsequently sends The Looper to retrieve one of the chips implanted in the heads of Doom's Henchmen for analysis, noting that they no longer have to worry about how to defeat Doom, since they have to find him first.

Jones and The Looper continue their search for Doom's whereabouts, they search around the many dig sites guarded by Doom's henchman, as they speculate there must be a paper trail that could lead them to Doom, The Looper takes down a henchman and collects a shipping manifest. Jones then sends The Looper to go talk with War Machine who has been analyzing some henchman notes, he discovers that Doom is incorporating parts of Pandora's Box into his new suit, he tells The Looper to start training and allows them to use some of his gear such as War Machine's Arsenal and War Machine's Hover Jets. Elsewhere, Hope desperate and nervous askes The Oracle if she has foreseen anything new, The Oracle talks about a feline ally, a cunning plan and a victory hidden in defeat. Jones finds Shuri, the feline ally The Oracle spoke of, as he explains Shuri's plan on taking down Doombot.

Hope tasks The Looper with collecting supplies for Shuri, while Jones fully explains the plan of reversing the energetic flow of the Rift Beacons with Rift Recalibrators so they can possibly send Doom somewhere, far away from the Island but they aren't sure if this plan can work so Hope suggests they try this plan on Doombot first. Hope starts to have doubts on herself thinking she might not be good enough to lead the war against Doom. Elsewhere The Looper hands over the parts they gathered to Shuri, she attaches the new Rift Recalibrators onto three Rift Beacons around Castle Doom. Hope sends in The Looper to defeat Doombot, but something goes wrong as the recalibrators start to short circuit. Hope and Shuri soon find out that Doom's lieutenants Mysterio and Emma Frost were the ones who tampered with the Rift Beacons, Hope realizes they can't just fight Doom and get it over with, instead they need to get rid of his lieutenants first.

Hope and Jones scout out Doomstadt via The Oracle's guidance, and discover that Mysterio has been mind controlling the people of Latveria, the wastelanders and their own henchmen in order to create the illusion of perfection.

The Looper is tasked with going around the island freeing everyone under Mysterio's control, before taking out the many security cameras around Doomstadt, leaving Mysterio with no other way of controlling the people of Latveria. With everyone now free, Hope with The Looper's and Jones' help went to confront Mysterio. After a long fight The Underground succeeds in liberating the people of Doomstadt, spraying graffiti around the town and on the statue of Doom.

Jones sends The Looper to talk with Iron Man, and Tony allows The Looper and The Underground to use his tech as they begin to make up a plan to defeat Emma Frost. After a test of Stark Tech, The Looper is sent to fly over Castle Doom to check the Rift Recalibrator signal while Tony gave power dampeners, to reduce Emma's psychic influence, to Hope. Jones then sends The Looper to test again the power of Stark Tech. Later, as Doom's army began to recover the Arcane Artifacts, which are fragments of Pandora's Box that Doom wants to use for his new armor, all over the Island, Doom's Chosen emerges to collect them and bring them to Doom. These six artifacts being intended to complete Doom's Pandora Armor, triggering a great battle between heroes and villains dubbed as the Day of Doom.

At Hope's request, mission details have been encrypted and classified and instructions have been hidden for The Looper on the Island. After The Looper found the phone, they got a call from Hope on a securised channel and she told them the plan: using the Stark Power Dampeners to reduce Emma's psychic powers and then launch an assault against her. The Looper first used Forecast Towers to locate Emma Frost's operatives and learned they were already looking for them. Then they hired a specialist on order to make sure everything was good to place the power dampeners around The Raft. Once done, Hope switched The Looper back to the main communication channel and was intending to coordinate an attack along with Jones and the other allies. However the Rift Recalibrators detected Doom's signal, and he was heading toward The Raft meaning he finished his new armor. Jones warned Hope about this, but was caught in a moment of panic forcing The Looper to face Emma Frost alone, but they managed to defeat her.

Absolute Doom Event[]

“
T minus five, four, three, two... Let him have it!
”
— Hope

Hope, Jones, and their allies brace themselves for Doom's imminent arrival, setting up Rift Beacons near the Central Lake with the plan to send him back to his home reality. Peelverine is left in charge of activating the beacons, but he quickly delegates the task to The Looper. As The Looper activates the beacons one by one, they all begin to aim towards Doom's Arcane Forge.

As the final beacon fires at the forge, Hope activates the rift but Doom's glowing green head emerges from the Arcane Forge, that was shielded, unleashing a powerful energy burst that engulfs the surrounding area with a wall of debris. Emerging as a giant, Doom swiftly annihilates almost everyone who opposes him in the area. After Doom flickered War Machine aside, Jones hurls his shield at Doom, but it has no effect and Doom knocks him to the ground. With Doom poised to finish him off, Hope leaps into action, she grabs Jones's shield and shields them both as the King of Latveria launches energy blasts at them. In a bold move, Doom grabs Hope, bringing her face-to-face with him. She stabs him in the eye with the shield still charged with Pandora's magic, leaving Doom with a scar over his eye which enabled a weak point suppressing his physical immunity. However Hope injured her own arm in the process. Peelverine, Gwenpool, War Machine, Iron Man and Iron Spider—supported by an army of Loopers—charge in, engaging Doom in an epic battle at the Central Lake. Ultimately, the heroes and Loopers prevail, managing to weaken Doom enought to activate the final Rift Beacon, stripping him of his Pandora's Box powers and sending him back to his home reality.

Remix: The Prelude[]

After the defeat of Doom, several of the Rift Beacons were scattered across the Island, one landing in Restored Reels, where artwork of The Shark and The Agency were drawn. The Rift Beacon activated, summoning a Rift Butterfly brought New York City's Times Square from Earth to the Island, where Ice Spice and Snoop Dogg performed a concert featuring some of their songs before the Rift Butterfly transported them and the Loopers of the Island to an another reality.

Chapter 2 Remix

Chapter 2 Remix[]

Remix: The Finale[]

The Loopers of Helios were transported to an alternate reality similar to Chapter 2: Season 2, where the Island is controlled by musical artists Snoop Dogg, Eminem, Ice Spice, and Juice WRLD. After a month of exploring a remixed Apollo, the artists performed one large concert featuring several of their greatest hits before the Rift Butterfly sends The Looper through a wormhole where they are joined by Hope and Jones. Hope, Jones, the Battle Bus, and The Looper all go through the Rift in the wormhole, followed by a fragment of the Zero Point, taking them all to Reality: 783.

Chapter 6: Season 1

Chapter 6: Season 1[]

鬼HUNTERS Quests[]

After the fragment of the Zero Point crashed onto Oninoshima, it created a reality wave which opened portals across the Island to the Spirit Realm, allowing the the ancient enemy of this land known as the Demon Warriors to invade the Island once again.

Shogun X and his demonic forces later took over the broken shard of the Zero Point and transported it in the Spirit Realm, to their master. Though Shogun X empowered himself with it first. Shogun X later emerged from the Spirit Realm to help The Night Rose fight off Hope and Kendo. After the fight, he went to wander across the Island to collect spirit energy and Sprites.

Now that the Zero Point Shard has restored the magic of Oninoshima, the Shattering War begins to repeat itself - with Daigo determined to continue work on the magical masks of old through dark magic and his ownership of The Thousand Faces to harness the power needed to enter the Demon Warriors' Spirit Realm for himself.

Later, Kendo, one of Hope's new associates, enlists the help of The Looper to investigate the resurgence of demons on the Island. The Looper agrees and begins hunting the Demon Warriors.

They are soon contacted by Kendo's sister: Jade, who is also working with Hope. The latter seeks to learn more about the Island's magic. After helping her place Spirit Charms across the Island, The Looper collects demon essence to study their nature and delivers it to Hope. Hope reveals she is searching for an artifact akin to Pandora's Box, stating she wasn't surprised by the island's current situation - suspecting it to be connected with the reawakening of Oninoshima's myths and ancient magic, as well as the demons.

Later, The Looper travels to Battle's End alone to investigate the return of magic. Sensing ancient magical resonance, they deduce it is connected to the demons, their rage, and the Spirit Realm. They conclude that something ancient and powerful is stirring the Island's magic.

“
Jade's recklessness and Daigo's arrogance may be why Jade is trapped in the Spirit Realm. The thing is, only demons can cross into the Spirit Realm. So, how did this happen?
”
— Kendo

Sometime after, Kendo calls upon The Looper once more - reporting that Jade is missing. He suspects their older brother: Daigo, is involved as Daigo has been experimenting with dark magic. Kendo claims to have seen Jade through a portal but dismissed it as a hallucination. However, when The Looper looks through the portal, they also see her - and hear her mention a shard.

Kendo sends The Looper to investigate Daigo's shop: The Thousand Faces, where they discover an underground hideout. Inside, they find various masks imbued with traces of dark magic, a painting of Daigo giving a mask to Jade, and a machine pulsing with dark energy.

They notably examine a Sprite cage, a worrying sight to The Looper, as well as a broken Mask of Power from long ago - though Kendo warns The Looper that the mask should never be repaired, for both of them view the masks as dangerous - thus Kendo stays away from using them, similarly to Mizuki.

Upon confronting Daigo, The Looper learns that Jade had actually been cursed by the demons, and that Daigo created a mask to protect her. However, the mask amplified her powers, trapping her in the Spirit Realm. Despite his regrets, Daigo insists he made the mask to keep her safe. Upon gathering all the information they could, The Looper reports their findings back to Kendo.

After sharing his suspicions about the artifact that Hope was seeking, Kendo tasked The Looper with investigating the comet. However, The Looper discovered that the comet had been stolen by demons. Continuing their investigation into the connection between the comet and the demons, The Looper and Kendo found a Splinter of the shard. Kendo instructed The Looper to deliver it to Hope, who then urged them to search for additional Splinters.

To gather clues about the other Splinters, The Looper consulted the Tanukis of Nightshift Forest. The Tanukis agreed to provide information if The Looper solved their riddles. After successfully completing the riddles, they led The Looper to a Splinter at the center of the forest.

Understanding the connection between demons and the Splinters, Hope asked The Looper to confront demon warriors to gather further clues. This led to the discovery of a map pointing to a third Splinter. Hope then realized that these fragments were actually pieces of The Zero Point and that they were crucial to saving Jade.

Kendo directed The Looper to Demon's Dojo to use the Spirit Forge and craft Blades imbued with the Splinters. However, uncertain about how to harness their power, Kendo suggested seeking assistance from The Night Rose. To gain access to her help, The Looper had to temporarily defeat The Puppeteer.

“
These blades, they carry the comet's strange power. This could free me... and, perhaps, your Jade.
”
— The Night Rose

Once freed, The Night Rose revealed that she was bound by a powerful demon and needed permanent liberation to provide answers regarding the comet and the Spirit Realms. She recognized the potential of the Splinter Blades, judging them strong enough to free both her and Jade. Before departing, she entrusted the Splinter Blades to Daigo.

Hope later requested The Looper's assistance in locating the comet. Their investigation revealed that someone had moved it to the Frozen Spirit Realm portal. Kendo, pondering the portal site, revealed that this was part of Daigo's plan to rescue Jade and that he had received the Splinter Blades from The Night Rose. Kendo emphasized the importance of understanding the link between the Spirit Realm, the comet, and the demons.

“
The comet that arrived on this Island... It's a piece of something greater. A shard of something called the 'Zero Point'.
”
— Hope

Hope called upon The Looper once more, sharing her theory about the source of Shogun X's immense power. She requested The Looper to fight Shogun X and bring back part of his essence. After completing this mission, Hope, who had not learned about The Looper's past from Jones, revealed the truth—the comet was a fragment of The Zero Point. Though The Looper already knew this, it confirmed the artifact's pivotal role in their quest.

Daigo heads to the frozen Spirit Realm portal with the Splinter blades of The Zero Point granted to him by The Night Rose. He begins his plans to free Jade from the Spirit Realm for good, determined to not let anybody stop him.

Utilizing his chaos magic, Daigo sits upon a magical orb-like aura and activates one of his six powerful masks onto the portal, causing him to be able to feel its power. However, an unidentified being, perhaps an illusion cast by something, tried to trick Daigo by taking the form of Jade, but failed. During this time, Daigo talked with several persons, including Hope, telling her they're similar as well as Jones, asking him about his motivations. He also began to feel a "dark presence" in the spirit realm and asked The Night Rose what it was.

“
The dark presence within the Spirit Realm is powerful. I can feel its malice. I can hear its whispers! It knows me, my family, our bloodline. It wants to take me too.
”
— Daigo

As Daigo's ritual progresses, he continues to be tormented by an entity taking the form of Jade. However, he refuses to be deceived by it. He warns his brother and The Looper about the dark presence lurking within the Spirit Realm, describing it as powerful and filled with malice. During a conversation with Jones, Daigo speaks about his motivations regarding the Shard, while also confiding in Hope about the immense pressure he feels from being "special."

Later, the entity resembling Jade ceases its torment, but Daigo reveals to The Looper and Kendo that the dark presence has begun speaking to him. This mysterious being appears to know him, his family, and his entire bloodline. Through this revelation, Daigo comes to understand that Shogun X, who had previously bound The Night Rose, is himself bound to that dark force.

Driven by pride, Daigo believes he alone is strong enough to face the darkness within the Spirit Realm, a conviction he shares with The Night Rose. He later confesses to Hope that, as the eldest son and a genius, he feels responsible for fixing everything. In his ongoing discussion with Jones, he acknowledges the seductive power of the Shard and questions whether Jones is truly capable of resisting its influence, subtly revealing his own doubts about him.

“
Soon, I'll be able to breach the Spirit Realm and save my sister... but that's not enough.
”
— Daigo

As the ritual nears its conclusion, Daigo shares his plan with Kendo and speaks about what he intends to do afterward. He then confides in Hope, expressing his sense of responsibility not just for his family but also for The Island, noting that she likely feels the same way. Turning to Jones, he challenges him, asking whether he will lock away the power of the Shard or use it for his own ends. In a conversation with The Night Rose, Daigo reflects on how, in another life, they might have been allies—or even friends. On the final day, Daigo speaks with Hope, questioning whether they will be friends or enemies in the near future. He then bluntly tells Jones that he does not trust him and refuses to let him near the Shard. Before the confrontation begins, he asks The Night Rose to watch over his siblings. Later, Daigo gifts The Looper magical items for battle, as his Chaos magic thrives on conflict.

The Confrontation Event[]

Armed with the Kintsugi Mask forged from the six other masks of the ritual, and the Splinter Blades crafted from the splinters of The Zero Point by The Looper and granted to him by The Night Rose, Daigo unfolds its magic in the the Spirit Realm portal to pull Jade back into the physical world. However, before he can succeed, Jade is violently dragged back into the Realm, and Shogun X emerges from the portal to stop him. Shogun X seizes Daigo by the face, triggering the power of his mask, which causes both of them to grow into towering forms. Shogun X then brutally assaults Daigo, absorbing energy from those around him through his Void magic and the open Spirit Realm portal. Despite the overwhelming power difference, Daigo seizes an opening and strikes back.

A fierce battle of swordplay and magic unfolds, with Shogun X maintaining the upper hand. When Daigo abandons his blades and attempts to use his Explosive Screaming Masks, Shogun X skillfully dodges and lands a devastating slash, mortally wounding him. As Daigo struggles, Shogun X grabs him by the mask and begins absorbing energy once more, preparing to finish him. In a last, desperate move, Daigo fuses his Splinter Blades into one and slashes through Shogun X's arm, freeing himself. With a final, decisive strike, he cuts through his enemy, dealing a fatal blow. Shogun X's accumulated energy erupts in a violent explosion, tearing through the portal and sending a shockwave that blasts Daigo away.

In the aftermath, the Spirit Realm portal glows gold, releasing waves of radiant energy. Freed from the dark presence, Jade finally escapes.

Jade is finally freed and immediately asks to see The Looper to discuss what comes next. She reveals that demons tried to break her but ultimately failed. However, she warns that the Dark Presence now possesses the Shard and has a plan. She expresses doubt that either of her brothers is strong enough to stop it, though she is particularly concerned for Daigo. She also reveals that the Dark Presence seeks to claim everyone—but especially Daigo—though she does not yet understand why.

“
The Spirit Realm wasn't dark, or evil... but there was a dark presence there. It has the Shard. We can't let it take the Island.
”
— Jade

Meanwhile, The Night Rose is freed from her curse and begins considering training new warriors, just as she once did for Shogun X's army before he bound her. However, both she and Jade caution The Looper about the golden energy emerging from the portal, warning that it will bring greed, conflict, and chaos.

As Kendo celebrates Jade's return, he feels indebted to The Night Rose and asks how he can repay her. At the same time, the siblings attempt to convince Daigo that he has done enough, fearing that his reckless pursuit of his goal will lead to disaster, only for Daigo to go missing. Kendo, for the first time, fully understands that Daigo is not just a frustrated genius—he is someone who has spent his life protecting his siblings at any cost. Elsewhere, Hope and Jones prepare for dark and dangerous times ahead now that the demons have the Shard. The Night Rose shares their concerns, while Jones, for now, chooses to trust her. On the side of the Demon Warriors, a new Shogun is elected to take the place of Shogun X. In a chilling display of loyalty, the new leader not only continues Shogun X's mission but also takes up his very name, ensuring his legacy of war endures. Just like his predecessor, he was empowered.

Chapter 6: Season 2

Chapter 6: Season 2[]

Lawless Quests[]

“
If you want to become wealthy beyond imagining... bring me the man with the golden hands.
”
— Fletcher Kane

The magical gold spread throughout the land, transforming Nightshift Forest into mountain of gold. Fletcher Kane and his Alpha mafia group quickly seized all the gold, using it to monopolize everything, starting a large gold mining operation in Shiny Shafts, transporting large amounts of gold in Armored Transports and the Train, and storing it all in banks and stashes all across the Island, including Crime City and the home of Kane, Lonewolf Lair.

“
We need to find Zero Point Shard before Kane and Daigo. They'll fritter its power away on small-minded ideas.
”
— Midas

Despite Kane's power on the Island, the massive amount of gold drew in several criminals known as the Outlaws. They would all rob Kane of the gold in his several vaults on the Island, and also created Black Markets hidden from Kane's greedy eyes. Although they were originally loosely associated, they became fully united under the leadership of Midas, who arrived on Oninoshima after learning that Kane, who was an old acquaintance of his until they had a falling out, had been making power moves and became a kingpin on this Island. However, Midas wasn't just there to stop Kane, as he had learned about the Zero Point Shard that landed on the Island and was taken into the Spirit Realm and had his own intentions to take it for himself, but was willing to work with Hope and Jones should he need to.

Daigo's Fate Event[]

“
We're proceeding to the portal phase shortly.
It's all over. They have no idea.
”
— Fletcher Kane

With his hatred of the Outlaws, Fletcher Kane had made a deal with Daigo, plotting to work together to obtain the Zero Point Shard. The deal was that Kane would give Daigo as much gold as he wanted and Daigo would in return create more magical masks for Kane's Alpha Guards. As well as this, they both were working together in an attempt to find a way into the Spirit Realm and obtain the Zero Point Shard. Returning to his workshop in Masked Meadows, Daigo using the gold given to him and the knowledge he had of creating magical masks to repair one of the destroyed Masks of Power which its remains were kept from the Shattering War. Daigo, using the ruinous Mask of Power, began using it at Magic Mosses, attempting to tear his way into a Spirit Realm Portal while Fletcher Kane guards him. Despite any attempts to stop or delay them, Daigo finally managed to break the seal that kept humans from entering the spirit realm, and ran into the portal, which resulted in him becoming corrupted into a Demon and being controlled by The Puppeteer. Meanwhile, a mysterious space station in a shape similar to a small moon had appeared in the sky and slowly began getting closer to the Island.

Galactic Battle

Galactic Battle[]

Imperial Takeover[]

The Galactic Empire arrived at Oninoshima from a galaxy far, far away, bringing with it a fleet of Imperial Star Destroyers, and the planet-destroying Death Star. Ruled by the power of Emperor Palpatine, they seek to take control of the entire Island. A First Order Base was built in the northeast mountains, where TIE Fighters are constructed under the command of Captain Phasma. Darth Vader and his Stormtrooper Samurai took control of the southwest Crimson Mountain, transforming it into his own Solitude. Hopeful Heights turned into a desert Outpost for smugglers. Hope and Jones have joined the Resistance, creating a Resistance Base in the northwest of the Island and building X-Wings to battle against the Empire. Heroes and villains from across eras have begun battle and the inhabitants of Oninoshima must choose between the Light Side or the Dark Side of the Force to determine the fate of the Island that hangs in the balance.

The Pull of the Force[]

The Rebel leader Leia Organa, the smuggler Han Solo, and the Jedi Luke Skywalker have arrived on the Island, assisting the Resistance in fighting the Galactic Empire, by investigating the intentions of the Imperial takeover as well as hiring and training any recruits of the Resistance. Meanwhile, the Jedi and Sith and recruiting more fighters to their cause, training them in the ways of the Force. As Rey and Mace Windu attempt to recruit more Jedi to the light, Emperor Palpatine, Darth Maul, and Darth Vader attempt to recruit more Sith to the Dark Side of the Force.

Mandalorian Rising[]

As the battle for Oninoshima continues, with both sides gaining more power and investigating ways to defeat their enemy, a group of Mandalorian Warriors have arrived on the Island, intervening with the battle in a search to prove the loyalty of their latest associates. The Looper, in an attempt to gain the Mandalorians' trust and ally with them, investigates and discovers that the associates have sided with the Empire and intended to betray the Mandalorians. With their suspicions confirmed, the Mandalorians consider taking on a new contract with more trustworthy associates.

Star Destroyer Bombardment[]

With the Resistance able to combat the Empire with their firepower and might, Palpatine fell to more drastic measures. Sending a Star Destroyer over the Island, the capital ship's Turbolaser could lay waste to the Island from above, able to destroy locations with a single shot. With time running out for the Resistance, Leia began to supervise and finalize a counterattack plan against the Empire.

Death Star Sabotage[]

“
JONES: You don't need to see my identification.
STORMTROOPER: What!?
”
— Death Star Sabotage

While the Death Star's superlaser was fully charged and aimed directly at the Island, the Resistance's plan finally came to fruition. Led by Hope and Jones, the Island's inhabitants posed as pilots of the Empire with TIE Fighters and captured X-wings and infiltrated the Death Star. Disguising as Stormtroopers with prisoners, they passed through the Death Star undetected. Once they were all grouped up, they made their way to the generator room and blasted all the generators, disabling the Death Star's weaponry and allowing an X-wing squadron to begin their final attack run.

The inside team were discovered and blasted their way through the Death Star until they encountered Emperor Palpatine. Using his powers, the Sith Lord disarmed the rebels of their weapons and began eliminating them. Before everyone could be eliminated, Hope threw a Rift-To-Go into Palpatine's Sith Lightning, causing everyone to rift away.

“
HOPE: Great job, team!
JONES: That is...not good.
”
— Death Star Sabotage

As Hope, Jones, the Looper, and any other survivors fell towards the Island, they watched as the Death Star's superlaser prepared to fire. Luckily, it was stopped last second when the X-wing squadron blow up the Death Star, sending its rubble crash landing onto the Island. A piece of the falling debris hit a Spirit Realm Portal that was being repaired with magical energy, sending a beam into the sky and releasing a shockwave that knocked the Looper out as they crashed onto the Island. As they were unconscious, energy emitted from their body. Meanwhile, the effects of the Portal attracted the attention of a superhero from space.

Chapter 6: Season 3

Chapter 6: Season 3[]

Shadows of the Shard[]

“
That creature... He talks like Daigo. He moves like Daigo. But there's something... wrong.
”
— Kendo

The Mask Maker Daigo returned to Oninoshima, possessed by the demonic forces from within the Spirit Realm. Seeking to drain the Island of its energy, he turned his home of Masked Meadows into Demon's Domain, summoning Spires to aid his goal. However, due to the influx of energy that surged from the Spirit Realm Portal, many of the Island's inhabitants were gifted unbridled power. Hoping to train the new superpowered individuals and help them master their new abilities to battle Daigo, Hope, Jones, and the Night Rose built Supernova Academy, and gained the help of Superman and Robin.

A Golden Shadow[]

“
I'm impressed. Yeah, Midas is bankrolling Supernova Academy... and our other special project, the Vault.
”
— Hope

While The Looper trained to hone the powers of Morgan Myst, a student who had confidence issues but leader potential similar to Hope, they discovered that Hope and Jones were hiding something in secret. Investigating clues in their workspace, it was discovered that they were working with Midas, who had been funding Supernova Academy and helped Hope in creating a Vault in Utopia City to contain the Zero Point Shard once it was obtained. Midas was untrustworthy, however, still working with his crew and the Outlaws to obtain the Shard for his own intentions.

The Fall of Supernova Academy[]

“
These Spires are woven from the magics of the Spirit Realm. Their master seeks to gain purchase in this world. We cannot let that happen.
”
— The Night Rose

Daigo and his army launched a surprise attack on Supernova Academy. The Supernovas managed to escape, however the Academy was left in ruins. As Daigo's influence spread further across the Island and Hope and Superman took respite in the Fortress of Solitude, Morgan Myst managed to gain her confidence as a leader, collecting mementos to help gain the confidence of the rest of the Supernovas. Using spire remnants, the Supernova symbol was constructed at the damaged academy as a symbol to the Island that the Supernovas can't be easily defeated.

Super Showdown[]

“
The power of the mask is mine to wield.
This is my destiny.
”
— Mask Maker Daigo

Mask Maker Daigo performed his ritual, aiming to achieve what he viewed as his ultimate destiny and summoned his master before being violently discarded, the Supernovas emerged into Demon's Domain with Formation Tau, consisting of the Morgan Myst, Lightrider and Killswitch and Formation Theta, consisting of Synthwave, Ziggy and Haylee Skye. The Looper and Daigo's siblings, Jade and Kendo and the other allies were also present at the scene, ready to fight the creature.

The Dark Presence's tentacle then proceeded to attack the heroes, using energy blasts to destroy them all. But through strategic use of their superpowers that they learned to master from their instructors, they're able to weaken the demon with a general weakening then fatal blows dealt by Supernovas at three specific moments, first with Synthwave and her sonic pulses, followed by Killswitch and his barrage of bullets, before Morgan finished the job with her swarm of Ravens, causing the tentacle, severely weakened, to retreat back into the portal.

Thinking they've won, their guard drops, leaving an open space for three separate tentacles to emerge from the portal, stronger than ever. With their energy pulse, the tentacles lock everyone in the air, while two of them converged their power towards the third, ready to annihilate them all in a single blast. However, the team's hope returns as Superman intervenes, cutting the tentacle that was going to attack, leaving it crashing to the ground, stopping for a second to greet The Looper before using his laser beams on the second tentacle and hitting the third one to return both of them to the Spirit Realm. He then destroyed the Spires to close the portal for good and left.

Epilogue[]

The other Monolith Spires became inactive, and Jade and Kendo celebrate the return of their brother, restored to his previous form, though he's going to need help to get back up from Night Rose and the others after what happened to him. However, the cut-off tentacle outside of Demon's Domain remained alive for a time and prepared something, while the heroes know this isn't the end and that they must recover from The Shard. In the meantime, the dark presence tentacle's started to rot, boiling from the inside and to spread its blood in the ground. Surrounding the area, bug holes emerge from the wounds of the tentacle, which expel small, purple glowing bugs. Then, the gigantic Spires reactivated again.

Chapter 6: Season 4

Chapter 6: Season 4[]

Welcome to the Infestation[]

“
The tentacle and the bugs are able to get to us from the Spirit Realm without using a portal. We're trying to figure out exactly how they do that. And where.
”
— Jones

Following the Super Showdown between The Supernovas and the Dark Presence from the spirit realm, the rotting tentacle left over from the battle gave birth to a massive Swarm, driving a island-wide infestation on Oninoshima. As the Swarm destroyed Lonewolf Lair, creating their Hive over it, the Onimorph eXtermination Rangers, or O.X.R., established themselves on the island as a military force to combat the invasion, setting up an HQ on the island. Their battle caused serious damage to the island, demolishing Crime City, a Ranger's Ruin remaining in its place, Demon's Domain, creating a hive over the debris, Brutal Boxcars, leaving a Swarmy Stash of leftover bugs, and Pumped Power.

The Looper met with the general of O.X.R., Onyx Winter, and was recruited as a member of the faction, helping to fight the Swarm alongside Lt. Ripp Slade, Agent Patch, Yoo-mi, and Mizuki. Wanting to help defend their home as well, Kendo, Jade, The Night Rose and Daigo have all begun battle with the bugs at different O.X.R. Bases around the Island. Meanwhile, Hope and Jones continued their research at Supernova Academy, investigating the tentacle, and determining how the Swarm access the Island from the Spirit Realm.

Egg-cellent Observations[]

As The Swarm begins to regain ground, invading Big Bend and creating a new nest Hive Hideaway, Hope asked The Looper to take out Swarmer Nests, to thin them out as much as they can. However, The Swarm appears to emerge directly from the Spirit Realm at an alarming rate and will soon overrun the Island. Seeking to know exactly how The Swarm came from, Hope asked The Looper to investigate its source, hoping to find the means The Swarm uses to get here from the Spirit Realm. Observing that the demonic energy is off the charts, The Looper decides to tune up sensors at Hope's request, thinking the nest must be close to their access point.

However, instead of discovering the access point, The Looper discovered some eggs with strange readings. Noting that the eggs were tainted with something, Hope asked The Looper to collect some and bring them to her to study them. She theorizes that the eggs are Queen eggs laid in the Spirit Realm and brought here, and that their energy signature could weaken the access point or even allow them to use the access point to their advantage to access the Spirit Realm without a portal. To confirm her theory, Hope sent The Looper to fight and destroy a Queen to retrieve data.

Dino Megazord Rises[]

In order to help fight The Swarm, the Mighty Morphin Power Rangers team intervened on the Island. Blue Ranger started to study the effectiveness of Precision Air Strike on The Swarm, Yellow Ranger started to use her Zord as transport to first aid, Black Ranger was training reinforcements, Pink Ranger was in aerial combat while Red Ranger was tied up fighting Queens. After getting some help from The Looper and other soldiers, they managed to assemble. However, before the Red Ranger could be summoned, Lord Zedd cursed Oninoshima and empowered The Storm. Then, when the Megazord was finally assembled, Rita Repulsa summoned a Queen and her army in an attempt to destroy it.

The Swarm's disappearance[]

With the power of the Dino Megazord, the O.X.R. finally turned the tide of the war against the Swarm. They reclaimed all their lost bases—including Ranger's Ruin and Swarmy Stash—and eradicated the remaining nests across the island after a final decisive battle at The Hive.[42]

With the Swarm believed to be defeated for good, the island entered a period of peace. This respite allowed the Supernovas to restore both the Supernova Academy and Utopia City. Meanwhile, the O.X.R. undertook extensive reconstruction efforts, rebuilding their ruined bases. Ranger's Ruin and Swarmy Stash were transformed into Ranger's Rest and Harmony Hills. The organization also terraformed the former large "Swarm" biome, replanting trees, rebuilding paths and roads, and converting what was once The Hive into Bugswatter Beach, a museum commemorating the war against the Swarm. On display at the museum is the Dino Megazord's Power Sword, serving as both a tribute and a reminder of the battle that saved the Island.

However, doubts soon began to surface regarding the island's fragile peace. Hope and Jones prepared to confront the Dark Presence, mobilizing the O.X.R. assets, which had been placed under their command by General Onyx Winter. The general maintained that the Swarm could never truly be defeated as long as their master still existed. Lieutenant Ripp Slade warned that the peace was nothing more than the calm before the storm, while Doctor Theodore Flyesbee theorized that the Swarm would not vanish on its own—unless something "much higher in the food chain" was approaching.

Afterward, a summit was held between the Island's leaders and allies to establish a definitive strategy for counterattacking the Spirit Realm and the Dark Presence—one that would involve the Mighty Morphin Power Rangers and their Megazord in a central role.[43]


Prepare to Fight[]

“
We have the Splinter Blades, and the old Swarm tunnels, and a small army of battle-tested allies. We've been in worse situations before, so let's do what we always do.
”
— Hope

Following the preparations for the assault on the Spirit Realm, Hope tasked The Looper with consulting The Night Rose, Jade, and Daigo. The Night Rose admitted that the plan was far from ideal, but also that there was no better alternative. Jade, on the other hand, compared the idea of attacking the Dark Presence with the Splinter Blades to "using a toothpick against a Great Turtle," though she conceded that luck might still favor them.

In the end, The Looper met with Daigo, who seemed to already know why they had come—to retrieve the Splinter Blades. Acknowledging his past failure, he handed the daggers to The Looper and asked them to finish what he could not: to pierce the veil and save everyone from the demonic incursion.

With the Splinter Blades recovered, Hope assigned The Looper a new mission—to rally the troops, secure ground support, and gather as much intel as possible. Once everything was in place, Hope warned that they were about to strike the access point and instructed The Looper to prepare their weapons. After running final tests, The Looper used the Splinter Blades to weaken the access point, before signaling to Hope that the path was clear.

Hope then declared that with the veil between realms now weakened, they had everything they needed to cross over—the blades, the Swarm tunnels, and a small army—before rallying everyone with a final message: they would need to stay focused, stand together, and make every action count.

Welcome, Our Alien Overlords[]

“
Alright, time to seal the breach and trap Mr. Demon in the spirit realm
”
— Jones

A cel-shaded sprout mysteriously appeared on the Island, eventually growing and turning into a treehouse. Meanwhile, a duo of aliens in a spaceship arrived on the Island to observe the strange anomaly. As Hope and Jones arrived to investigate, the treehouse was releasing an aura, causing a tear between the Island and the spirit realm. Jones, using his upgraded rift device, attempted to seal the tear. After what appeared to be a success, the tear reopened large enough for the Dark Presence to emerge from it and attack the Island.

The aliens, Kang and Kodos, infatuated by the Dark Presence, offered it their assistance, attacking the Loopers and abducting Hope and Jones in their spaceship. Hope managed to take control of the ship, attacking the Dark Presence. In an attempt to remove the Zero Point Shard from the demon with the spaceship's tractor beam, a smaller shard was torn off before the spaceship was sent through an inter-reality wormhole. As the aliens attempted to redirect the wormhole to Springfield, Hope battled with the calibration to return to the Island, resulting in the merging of realities into Springfield Island. The Battle Bus crashed into the spaceship, sending Hope Jones, and the smaller Zero Point Shard to the new Island.

Chapter 6: Mini Season 2

Chapter 6: Mini Season 2[]

Apocalypse D'Oh[]

The Zero Point Shard crashed into the Simpsons' home in Evergreen Terrace. Homer Simpson, learning that the fragment was powerful, fused the fragment into his TV remote, allowing the remote to bend reality to whatever Homer decides. As Homer began using the shard in his attempt to make the Springfield Island a paradise, Hope and Jones began exploring Springfield Island while cleaning up Homer's mess in the process.

Hope attempted to communicate with the people of the Island, only for them to be aggressive towards her. Confronting Homer at the Springfield Nuclear Power Plant, Homer claimed he would give up the power should they help him with specific tasks. After completing a Super Squishee delivery from the Kwik-E-Mart at Springfield Town Square and raising concerns with Townhall at Corruption Corners, Homer gave permission to have his house searched, only to be revealed that the shard was not there, and Homer would not give it up so easily.

Sugar High[]

Professor Frink examined Homer's remote, explaining the omniversal power of the shard fused to it. Homer, still convinced he's making Springfield a paradise, used the remote to transform a cloud in the sky into a giant donut, and Frink into Peelfessor Frink.

At first, Homer's idea didn't seem too bad to the eyes of the town, but the continuous food rain created by Homer proved to only create chaos. After Homer is delivered several angry notes from the people of Springfield about the anomalies caused by the Zero Point Shard, Homer had a new idea: to get rid of the evidence.

Multiplidiocy[]

With all of Springfield against Homer's actions, Homer attempted to rid the Zero Point Shard by tossing it into the nuclear core vat of the Springfield Power Plant. However, distracted by an unfinished candy bar, Homer accidentally fell into the vat, causing a reaction that erupted the Power Plant and transformed Homer into countless clones that spread across Springfield Island.

Bart and Lisa Simpson both attempted their own ways of getting all of Homer's Clones' attention, by either playing music over Moe's in Springfield Town Square, spraying the El Barto logo around Springfield Island, or by trying to fill fast food restaurants with vegan foods. With the kids out of options, the only solution left was to eliminate the clones, which released gases into the air and formed a large, pink cloud, in the hopes that the real Homer's essence was within the cloud.

The Incredible Bulk[]

Once the Homer Clones were eliminated, the cloud, collecting the gases from the clones, transformed back into the real Homer Simpson, however bigger and in his underwear. Hope scanned Homer and revealed that the Zero Point Shard was inside him and was causing him to become a Titan. Hope, Jones, Marge, Bart, Lisa, and Peely followed Homer to the shores of Springfield Island. After Hope's plan to immobilize Homer failed, they decided to try Marge's plan to get the Zero Point Shard, which involved getting the help from Frink.

Frink's idea was to obtain his previous inventions, like the Hamburger Earmuffs and the Sarcasm Detector, and improve upon them. Meanwhile, Marge collected photos of their family and decorated it over a billboard as a message for Homer that the best way to make things better is to be with his family. Confronting the Titan in the water, Homer managed to burp out the Zero Point Shard, allowing Hope and Jones to collect it. Frink tried to use his de-bigulator to shrink Homer back to normal size, however it failed, concluding that there was still Zero Point energy inside him keeping him from going back to normal, giving Hope a plan to bring Homer with them back to Oninoshima to help battle against the Demon Master.

Zero Hour - Chapter 6 Finale
[]

“
If you can hear this message, WE NEED YOUR HELP. The Dark Presence thinks he can conquer our Island. Well, not without a fight. Prepare yourselves. We're about to go for a ride.
”
— Jones

Hope and Jones, with their upgraded Rift Device using Homer's Zero Point Shard, began to contact allies and forces across The Omniverse. Once they had enough reinforcements, Jones gave a speech to rally all the forces together, and they left Springfield Island.
