After a few minor errors in rift travel, they managed to arrive back on Oninoshima. The Island had been severely damaged by the Dark Presence. All of Oninoshima's greatest warriors, including Godzilla, Kong, the Resistance and Galactic Empire, Supernovas, Superman, Dino Megazord, Homer Simpson, and many more arrived through Rifts, and began battle against the Dark Presence and his army.

After a long battle for the Island, the Dark Presence was still too strong for the titans and the rest of Oninoshima's army. Jones, with one last crazy plan, used the Rift Device to summon a red cube, able to withstand a blast from the Dark Presence and counterattack it with its own energy beam. The Dark Presence attempted to destroy the Cube, however, before it can explode, Mecha Team Leader sent the Cube into the Demon Master with a powerful kick.

The explosion vanquished the Dark Presence, leaving only its Zero Point Shard. The shard, now freed, sent out a reality wave, returning all the warriors to their proper realities and restoring Oninoshima of the damage caused. Once complete, the shard was transported to a new reality, bringing Hope, Jones, and the Looper with it, leaving the Island to be protected by the Spirit Siblings, Kendo, Jade, and Daigo.

  • Chapter 7

Before Chapter Seven

Before Chapter Seven[]

After the Dark Presence's defeat on Oninoshima, Jones, Hope, and The Looper departed and traveled to the Golden Coast Island in Reality: 266. There, they settled and began working alongside InnoLoop concerning the Zero Shard. While the situation seemed initially peaceful, the Dark Voyager's spaceship suddenly arrived and quickly brought down the Battle Bus — and nearly the Bus Driver with it — before beginning to observe the Island. Meanwhile, Hope's visions went dark, leaving her dreamless. As both heroes worked to learn more about the Dark Voyager and his intentions, Jones began connecting him to events from his past in Reality Zero. In the meantime, Peely was cast in the film Peelian, directed by Kingston and produced by Sus Studios.

Chapter Seven: Season 1

Chapter Seven: Season 1[]

Pacific Break[]

“
Mission log. This reality feels familiar in so many ways, yet something's not quite right.
”
— The Order

The Order, one of The Seven, arrives on Reality: 266 after receiving a distress signal from Jones while analyzing the aftermath of the battle on Reality: 783. Using a pod encapsulated in a meteor similar to those previously deployed by The Seven, she lands near Classified Canyon.

Upon touching down on the Golden Coast, she records her first mission log, explaining her arrival and noting that the situation appears linked to the shattered Zero Point, as her scans reveal unusually high concentrations of Zero Point energy across the Island. She later travels to Storm Station, where she records a second mission log, observing that someone else is searching for the Zero Shards and that she must uncover their motives. Speaking to herself, she reflects on the fate of The Seven — who are no longer what they once were — before refocusing on the mission and beginning to scout the area.

Lethal Labs[]

After the Dark Voyager seized control of InnoLoop Labs and the Zero Shard, The Looper sought to infiltrate his ranks by sending a message via a holotable found in Lethal Labs, pledging their loyalty. Intrigued, the Dark Voyager assigned them missions to test their usefulness — notably to gather intelligence on the Zero Shards and The Seven. However, this proved to be nothing more than a ploy; the Dark Voyager used The Looper for his own ends before planning to dispose of them, claiming to prefer the loyalty of minions forged over aeons rather than that of a stranger offering to help. He dispatched his minions — Skulldroid and NITE Unit Sniper — to kill them, but The Looper swiftly defeated both in battle and escaped.

In the weeks that followed, The Looper got to know the Golden Coast's inhabitants and became involved in Sus Studios's newest film, Peelians, starring Peely. After testing the Island's equipment with Miles Cross and Carter Wu to hone their stunt skills, The Looper helped Cat Holloway step into the role of an action hero by sharing their own shooting techniques in exchange for some celebrity tips. At the same time, Winterfest celebrations began with the arrival of Lieutenant Winter — a veteran soldier and the long-standing face of Winterfest, recently promoted since his last tour, who had grown weary of being associated solely with the holiday.

In need of a break at the beach, Lieutenant Winter asked The Looper to help deliver the perfect gifts to everyone on the Golden Coast — including five minutes of well-earned silence for himself. Through a series of tasks — busting through doors for the perfect stunt, honing aim at Wonkeeland's Shooting Gallery, signing autographs for fans, and delivering gifts across the Island — The Looper bonded with the actors. As the release of Peelians drew near, The Looper wished Cat Holloway luck for her performance.

Setting the Stage with Kingston[]

While The Looper began assisting Kingston on the Peelian set — hiring new talent, distributing newspapers, and scouting new filming locations — the Dark Voyager, who had deployed a large-scale scan of the Island ever since seizing InnoLoop, began forming a golden rift above his ship. At the same time, The Order continued her mission and set up camp near Ol' Stumpy, where she recorded a new mission log. She noted that Reality: 266 feels strangely familiar, yet something seems off. The Loop appeared stable, but the Storm had changed — suggesting someone was tampering with it, or that it was a consequence of the fractured Zero Point. She expressed a wish that The Scientist were there to apply his intellect to the problem, but pressed on regardless.

Born In Chaos[]

In another reality, a group of unusual children got their hands on an artifact far beyond their control. When Eric Cartman and his friends excluded a member from their Fortnite party, they drew the wrath of Professor Chaos, who used the powerful Stick of Truth to force them to include him. Things quickly spiraled out of control — the artifact's cosmic power transformed the school bus into a Battle Bus and outfitted the kids with gear before rifting them to Reality: 266's Golden Coast, where Eric Cartman claimed Wonkeeland and renamed it Cartmanland.

The Order's Mission[]

“
Based on that ship of his, he's connected to the Last Reality. But something feels off. I think there's more going on than meets the eye.
”
— The Order

The Order infiltrated Lethal Labs, now overrun with thugs in service of the Dark Voyager. Leaving another mission log inside, she reported to Jones that she had secured a lock on the last components needed to build the device that would allow her to contact her allies. Before being abruptly interrupted and captured by the Dark Voyager, she noted that he must have a connection to The Last Reality based on the design of his ship — while remaining uncertain that there wasn't more to his origins than what met the eye. She was then imprisoned in Lethal Labs' secret cell — the very same one Carina had heard rumors about and shared with Cat Holloway. Shortly after, the Dark Voyager departed Reality: 266 with his ship.

The Reassembly Events[]

As Carina recruited The Looper to help uncover the truth, she directed them to investigate everything that remained mysterious — including the Storm and the Reboot Vans — while also confiding her wariness of Human Bill and the Henchfans. After having The Looper question Jones and Hope about the Dark Voyager on her behalf, both urged The Looper to persuade Carina to stay out of it, fearing for her safety. Meanwhile, the golden rift began to reform, and before long the Dark Voyager's ship emerged once more. Hope and Jones were stunned to learn he had already secured a second Zero Shard — the ease of it standing in stark contrast to the battles they had fought just to retrieve one — and began questioning the Dark Voyager's true identity and purpose. They told The Looper they weren't ready to confront him yet, but that they were building their forces in preparation for the fight ahead.

Breakpoint[]

As Jones informed The Looper of the coming war against the Dark Voyager and had them train with new weapons and gear, The Looper also explored Lethal Labs' underground facility — where they discovered The Order, imprisoned in the rumored secret cell and sealed inside a cubic barrier that prevented her from rifting out. She asked The Looper to find the blue-haired girl who had been poking around the labs and to retrieve the keycard she had found that would deactivate the barrier. In exchange, The Looper helped Carina track down Bigfoot and Human Bill, and collect gold bars on her behalf. Upon returning to The Order, The Looper, Jones, and Hope successfully freed the spy, allowing her to rift out in search of the remaining scattered members of The Seven — who would soon be needed for the coming fight. Her escape triggered the cell's alarms, bringing Skulldroid and NITE Unit Sniper rushing in to deal with the intruders, only to be defeated once again.

The Stranger[]

“
This Dark Voyager is dangerous.
And we don't know what he's planning to do with the Zero Point shards.
”
— The Order

The following week, The Looper contacted The Order, now patrolling at Classified Canyon. She tasked them with assisting her new mission: constructing a device to contact The Seven — or whoever remained. She explained that the plan was drawn from a database of emergency blueprints left behind by The Scientist, and that the beacon they would build was designed to reach any surviving allies. Following The Order's instructions, The Looper cleared a path for the signal and gathered the materials needed to build the transmitter, which was then installed under the antenna near the Battlewood sign. Once complete, The Order thanked them and assured The Looper that whoever answered the call, The Seven would soon stand against the Dark Voyager and put a stop to whatever he was planning with the Zero Shards. Jones also concluded his training regime, having The Looper sharpen their combat skills and clear debris at the Canyon to prepare a landing zone for incoming allies.

Visitor's Return[]

“
I don't know who will answer this call.
But soon enough, The Seven will stand against the Dark Voyager.
”
— The Order

After some time, a red rift laced with Havoc Energy tore open the sky above the Golden Coast. Jones, Hope, The Order, and The Looper finished preparing the landing zone just in time. As the rift began to glow intensely, a rocket burst through — generating a chain of rifts that gradually cleansed the vessel of the Havoc energy contaminating it. Following a series of complex maneuvers and rift jumps, and narrowly avoiding a collision with The Shape, the rocket stabilized and executed a safe landing on the newly constructed platform at Classified Canyon. The Visitor had arrived.

When The Order made contact with him, both quickly recognized they were not the individuals they had expected to meet. The Visitor deduced that they originated from different realities or divergent timelines. Despite this, he agreed to aid the heroes, affirming that regardless of when or where they came from, The Seven stand united. He was then brought to the main base, where The Order and the others briefed him on the current situation. In return, The Visitor shared his own experiences — recounting his pursuit of the Dark Voyager and the events that had led him there. He then enlisted The Looper's help in improving the new base by testing hiding spots, clearing threats at the Canyon, and evaluating the new Seven gear he had supplied.

Interference[]

“
Fascinating. You are not the Order I expected to meet when I picked up your distress call.
”
— The Visitor

Not long after his arrival, The Visitor grew increasingly uneasy with The Order's approach to investigating the Dark Voyager. Concerned by her methods, he asked The Looper to speak with her and persuade her to reconsider. The Order, however, instructed The Looper to disregard The Visitor's concerns. Using Stealth Splash as camouflage, The Order and The Looper attempted to outmaneuver the Dark Voyager. During the operation, The Order contacted The Visitor again, urging him to assist if he was truly so worried about the plan. He refused, insisting that confronting the Dark Voyager directly was unwise — especially since they still did not fully understand his capabilities. The Order countered that they would never learn what he was capable of without conducting reconnaissance.

As they began sabotaging the Dark Voyager's work at Lethal Labs, The Visitor reached out once more, ordering The Order to stay away from the enemy. She refused, stating she did not take orders from him. In response, The Visitor reminded her that he had pursued the Voyager through the husks of burned-out stars and the ashes of collapsing realities and that if he were so easily defeated, he would have already done so. The Order retorted that they had only just met and that she did not know his capabilities, before cutting off communication.

Later, after The Order dispatched The Looper to investigate the Shards, The Visitor contacted them again. He accused The Order of endangering the mission through her hubris and impatience, remarking that she had become reckless — just like her sister. The Order replied that The Imagined would have understood the stakes and expressed surprise that he did not. The Visitor then asked whether she had learned nothing from losing her. She answered that she had learned one thing: when everything is on the line, it is better to be reckless than timid. The Order then instructed The Looper to await her next call.

First Wave[]

Hope informs The Looper that she's been receiving coded transmissions from The Elites, a team of skilled fighters which Daigo, her former frenemy, is now also a part of. She tasks the hero with scouting The Basement as a possible base for The Elites, also telling them that if they were to impress the team, they may join the heroes in the fight to come.

Meanwhile, aboard the Dark Harvester, the Dark Voyager has kidnapped Fishstick in an attempt to extort information about his homeworld which is home to the third Zero Point Shard needed to assemble the fractured Zero Point. After locating Reality: 7021 among the countless realities in his map, the Dark Voyager opens a rift to reach the planet housing Atlantis and its inhabitants. The Zero Point Shard is pulled by the Dark Harvester from the bottom of the ocean, causing the city to dry up and the fisherman floating above it to be extracted with the artifact. Once inside the ship, the Dark Voyager sends his Trespassers to remove the intruder who is angrily looking at the confused Fishstick.

The Dark Voyager quickly returns to Reality: 266 and extracts the third shard he acquired from his ship, now to be placed with the others at Lethal Labs. Hope starts to worry about the ease with which the Dark Voyager has been able to gather three Zero Point Shards with his ship, comparing it with all the challenges she had to go through to simply retrieve one.

Final Wave[]

As the Second Wave concludes with the third Zero Point Shard placed with the others, the Dark Voyager enacts his Final Wave, starting to charge the Zero Point Shards with the Dark Harvester energy beam. Once charged, the ship shoots a final blow strong enough to generate Zero Point tendrils and opening three shard distortions at Clawsy Lodge, Bumpy Bay and near Looper's Leap. After the blow, the Dark Harvester shortly loses energy, but its systems are seemingly rebooted by Void Walker Lynx through a small Zero Point splinter allowing her master to observe the position of the distortions[44].

The Looper is quickly sent by Hope to investigate the distortions, while Peelians' actors start to feel that the Golden Coast is approaching dark times.

  7. ↑ "I'm watching you, traitor." - Jules to Brutus, Chapter 2: Season 6

  9. ↑ "Woah... are we back online?" -Special NPC dialogue from Meowscles to Midas in Chapter 3: Season 3
  10. ↑ Agent Jones Quests
  11. ↑ The Spire Quests
  12. ↑ The events of Chapter 2 Season 7 and Chapter 2: Season 8 are shown as taking place outside The Loop. Chapter 3 also begins by confirming that characters including The Looper are outside of The Loop without there being any logical opportunity to escape. Bunker Jonesy is also completely different from his Chapter 2: Season 5/Chapter 2: Season 6 counterparts, no longer recognizing The Looper at all and not talking about escaping The Loop.
  13. ↑ Foreshadowing Quests features speaking characters for radio broadcasts and others, meaning it can't be in The Loop
  14. ↑ "Doctor Slone needs an operative who's willing to stay off the books and do whatever it takes to secure the safety of the Island. Will you answer the call?" -Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 2's News Tab Description
  15. ↑ "The IO is compromised. You will find the mole." -???, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 2
  16. ↑ " Listen up. Your mission is to infiltrate Believer Beach. Find the ringleader and win them over." -Dr. Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 3
  17. ↑ "Between you an me - I know how to disrupt the mothership's communications." -Joey, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 3
  18. ↑ "Welcome back, agent. I need you to focus on Sunny. I think she may be behind the uptick in anti-human sentiment on the Island." -Dr. Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 5
  19. ↑ "Find a way to make contact. Peacefully." -Sunny, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 5
  20. ↑ "If they come in peace, we've gotta welcome in peace." -Sunny, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 5
  21. ↑ "We need a boots-on-the-ground presence on Hayseed's farm. We need him alive and on our side. -Dr. Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 6
  22. ↑ "The abduction situation is hitting a crisis point. We need to get a handle on it yesterday." -Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 6
  23. ↑ "Get familiar with the Recon Scanner. You'll need it to catch Riot. Great work. You're almost ready to take down that traitor, Riot." -Rook, Chapter 2: Season 7 Epic Quests Week 7
  24. ↑ "Command wants us to stop stalling. It's time to go after Riot. Great job sharpshooter! Command will be impressed." -Special Forces, Chapter 2: Season 7 Epic Quests Week 7
  25. ↑ "I need to pick up some 'off the record' IO chatter. They're talking about a mole! Just what I thought.The IO has a mole." -Doctor Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 8
  26. ↑ "The mole has leaked our operation. These civilians know too much." -Doctor Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 8
  27. ↑ "We might have more than a mole to contend with. An infiltrator... of the alien variety." -Doctor Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 8
  28. ↑ "We still have a significant lack of knowledge about the alien invasion force. Knowledge isn't just power here... knowledge is survival. We'll start by monitoring their activities on the Island." -Dr. Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 9
  29. ↑ " The rate of alien abductions is far above expectations. This isn't just for study. It's like they want to abduct everyone at least once." -Dr. Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 10
  30. ↑ "They're targeting the old Slurp factory for a reason. We'll find out soon enough. The aliens have taken an interest in Slurpy Swamp. Clear the area so the IO can investigate." -Doctor Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 10
  31. ↑ "I'm hitting a dead end. We need an asset on the inside. There's an alien in quote-unquote "deep cover." Try to squeeze any useful intel from them. If you can recruit them, even better." -Dr. Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 11
  32. ↑ "I'll do what I can to nudge my people in a different direction. But no promises." -Joey, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 11
  33. ↑ "The IO does have a counter attack plan in place, but I believe it's been compromised. You and me are creating a backup plan." -Doctor Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 12
  34. ↑ "Let's apply a little pressure. Make them feel... watched. Push them into making a big move." -Marigold, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 13
  35. ↑ "I need you to "leak" our secret base location. The mole should take it from there. I don't think they'll be able to resist this juicy intel - but I have a backup plan, just in case." -Marigold, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 13
  36. ↑ "Let's get some insurance in case our Mole is onto us. Share that intel with one of these contacts. I think that should do it for strategic intel leaks. Time for phase 2." -Marigold, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 13
  37. ↑ "If everything works like I think it will... that Mothership's days of ominous hovering are numbered." -Dr. Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 14
  38. ↑ "Unfortunately, The area where it's coming down isn't as unpopulated as I'd like. I need you to warn the people there ahead of time and get them to leave. Can't risk any of them doing something to interfere with the plan. -Dr. Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 14
  39. ↑ "The mole has activated the bomb too soon. Neutralize it. Quickly." -Doctor Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 14
  40. ↑ "Find Maven. Confront her. Make sure she knows her duplicity has consequences. There is no room at the IO for traitors. Maven will soon learn that." -Doctor Slone, Chapter 2: Season 7 Legendary Quests Week 14
  41. ↑ Chapter 5: Season 4 Story Quests
  42. ↑ "Those pesky Queens are no match for Megazord's might. The Power Rangers couldn't have done it without you!" -Login Reward message for Megazord's Might Loading Screen
  43. ↑ Spirit Realm Siege
  44. ↑ As seen in Dark Innovations loading screen, we can understand the Zero Shard allowed the Harvester to be instantly active right after the Story Moment concluded which showed the ship completely losing power
